To become a rustler you must acquire your guns and gear. There is an outer trail in which you are a small-time rustler and you steal cows and horses and put your gang together. There are 32 real outlaws with biographies in this game. Critters stolen in one state need to be driven to another for sale.  They can be stolen from you on this journey. Once you have at least two gang members you can move to the inner trail where the banks and trains are. These are the same ones robbed by the Wild Bunch.  Interspersed throughout the board are "Outlaw Fate" squares.  When you land on these you take an Outlaw Fate card.  This is real history.  There are 192 of these cards and each card tells a story and the resulting board move.  They can either be good, bad or neutral to you. Once you have stolen a predetermined amount of money you can take the "Final Trail" to win the game.  Tom Horn is waiting for you and may be you'll make it and maybe your won't.

boardgame: Disney's The Lion King Circle of Life Dominoes
description: Meet the endearing characters from Disney's The Lion King while playing the classic game of dominoes. It's a fun game that builds memory and matching skills. Players take turns flipping over dominoes and trying to match characters like Simba, Mufasa and Sarabi to dominoes already in The Circle of Life. The player who completes the three-foot-wide circle wins.

W3 Who What Where is a card-based storytelling game.  Players take turns to draw cards from a deck into their hands.  Their goal is to gather enough W3 cards (Who, What, Where, When) to allow them to spin a story about the printed object, event, location, and time.  The players then need to play a Story card to tell the tale and win the game.  Other cards enable players to stop a Story from being told, add on more goals needed for a Story to be successfully completed, and to trade cards with one another.
publishers: The United States Playing Card Company

description:
Part of the Bicycle Kids children's games from USPC.
Players have to collect sets comprising of three cards. A correct set will consist of a Head, a Body and the Feet (so the child can form a correct and complete image of the character).
publishers: The United States Playing Card Company

description: Hexa: Derwin Playing Cards with Hexadecimal Power (base 16).  This is a modified standard deck of cards using base 16.  There are 64 cards in the deck in four suits, plus two jesters.  It includes rules for several traditional card game variants, including poker, rummy, bridge, old maid, cribbage, blackjack and more.  Among the blurbs on the package: "Educational & Entertaining"; "Challenge your Math Skills"; "May be used with a Scientific Calculator".

This game appears to have come as an insert in a Swedish comic book devoted to the superhero The Phantom.  The game map shows the Phantom's Island with four bases in each corner of the map representing the bad guys' lairs (Hydra, Scorpion, Vrakarna, and Singh Pirates).  I do not have an English translation of the rules.  From what I can surmise, the four criminal gangs are trying to steal the Phantom's treasures from the island.  The hex-shaped counters are printed on the same insert as the mapsheet, and must be cut out and mounted prior to play.

From the publisher's web site...

Gummi Wars is the new micro-miniatures game that uses candy as miniature figures. All the thrills of fast-paced miniatures combat, with the added bonus that you can eat your dead. What could be better?

boardgame: Blow Me Out of the Sky
description:
From the publisher's web site...

"Funny yes, but this addictive micro-miniatures game will have you screaming for more. Man-to-man modern fighter combat using models, die-cast and plastic toys comes to life in this in-depth strategy game. Everything from basic combat maneuvers to varying armaments and payloads are covered in these fast-paced rules."
In the woods of the bunnies, after the apocalypse, the remaining mutated bunnies eat carrots, make more bunnies, frighten each other and fight. The game is in Swedish.

boardgame: ( Your Name Here ) of the Jungle
description:
From the publisher's web site...

"Man working in harmony with nature, life and death struggles, single-word sentences! Its all here in this game of strategic and tactical combat that uses plastic jungle toys as miniatures. An in-depth strategic struggle and resource management game that will challenge even the most veteran game player."

From the publisher's web site...

"Ever wonder what to do with that bucket of Plastic Infantry Guys? The answer's right here: Send them into battle! War PIGs is an easy to learn combat system for miniatures that uses plastic soldiers and poseable action figures. It's also an in-depth strategic and tactical conflict that will challenge even the most veteran miniatures enthusiast!"

boardgame: Quick Shtick: The Jewish Quick Thinking Game
description: Can you name: A Jewish food starting with "K"? A biblical character whose name starts with "R"? A Parsha beginning with the letter "B"? Quick Shtick is a fast-paced and exciting game that is great for parties. Players compete to guess the correct answer as quickly as possible before their opponents do. There are 40 Jewish categories to test your knowledge. Includes Gameboard, Instructions, Answer Booklet, Game Cards, Bag of Pawns and Die. 8 years to adult.

Grid Iron is the new, fast paced, action packed football game played with eight dice. With Grid Iron, you will not only be transformed into the Quarterback barking out the plays, but also the Coach, the Receiver and the Fullback barreling ahead for that First Down. You'll also get to make great defensive plays, like flying in as the Safety to make a leaping stab at the ball, or as the Linebacker coming in to make a crushing stop on the ball carrier. Grid Iron is an easy game to learn. Yet the combination of strategy, skill and luck makes every game played a pressure cooker. So if you can stand the pain, get ready to knock heads with the best of them for hours of fun and competition. Game includes eight dice, playing field, five game markers, score pad and instructions.

From the publisher's web site...

"Blazing Lasers! Titanic death-dealing dreadnoughts! Faster than light travel! None of these has anything to do with Space War!.... It's Los Angeles, city of six million cars and one lousy parking space."

From the publisher's web site...

"Play a gladiator, slave or even a lion as you do your best to win the favor of the Emperor in the Roman Coliseum. Combat isn't your only weapon, and remember, you don't have to run faster than the lion..."

From back of game:

Can you run faster than the lion?

Chris throught he could... so did Dan.

Who knows their fate?   Only you can decide.

Brunch at the Coliseum is a hilarious game of strategic and tactical survival for two or more players ages 12 and up.  Violent themes.

From the publisher's web site...

"Vicious, gigantic bugs want our candy bars and they're willing to invade Earth to get them! The arsenal of the entire planet has turned out to repel them... too bad it's the 1950s and that arsenal doesn't amount to much. Starship War PIGs pits plastic Bugs against plastic Infantry Guys in a titanic miniatures struggle for the mastery of Earth. "

The rich. The famous. The powerful. They're today's people—making today's news. Everyone wants to know as much about them as possible. And most likely, you're no exception. But how much do you  really  know about today's famous people? Enough to answer 3,000 trivia questions in categories like SONG, TUBE, SCREEN, PAGES, JOCKS, and CHATTER? Enough to bet on your knowledge—and move rapidly ahead? Enough to trick—and slow down—an opponent? Adapted from the ever-popular PEOPLE WEEKLY magazine, this fast-paced, fascinating game is sure to provide the answer whenever the question is fun. [Please note that this is not the same as the Patch Products game of the same name produced in 2002.]

The premise behind the game is matching one boxer and his Action Cards versus the other boxer and his Action Cards. Within each round of boxing each player will FLIP over 8 cards, one at a time, at the same time. Upon flipping each card both players should state the boxing move and the point total out loud. The higher number indicates which boxer wins the exchange. Each time a boxer wins an Exchange he gets closer to gaining a knockdown and closer to winning the round. To signify the winner of an exchange, advance the pawn one notch along the championship belt towards your opponent's KO area.

HEAVYWEIGHT BOXING is a two-player game with a playing time of about 30 minutes a bout. You are a manager controlling the ring strategy of your fighter, balancing his punching power and offensive and defensive boxing abilities against those of his opponent. You can play two contemporary (1979) or all-time-great fighters creating many "what-if?" situations.
This is not a long-winded, overly complex game but was in the vanguard of beer and pretzels games before the German games invasion. You should be able to read the rules and play your 1st bout within an hour. Advanced optional rules can be added later for more realistic flavor.
Components are meager but complete. Included is a simulated boxing ring with 2 corners divided into sections for each fighter's stance of Aggressive/Counter/Defense. Also included are 2 realistic metal fighters (look great if you paint them), 2 Game charts, and a pad of score sheets, rulebook, 1D6, 20 Contemporary and 20 All-time-great fighter cards.
A turn consists of simultaneously: 1) Secretly choosing your stance, 2) rolling on the "Boxing ability vs. stance" chart for any punches landed, 3) rolling for each landed punch on the "Punching power vs. defense" chart for results, 4) recording points and stats on the score sheet, 5) roll recovery if needed and repeat sequence 3x for each round.
Advanced optional rules include Endurance, TKO, Injuries, Solitaire, Rope-a-dope, Haymakers, Clinching, Stuns, Head Butts, Tiring and everyone's favorite; "add your own" rule.
This game has a real nice boxing feel to it and is remarkably easy to play, especially if you're not the bookkeeper statistician. If you are creative you can come up with modern fighter cards based on their stats.

boardgame: Von Manstein: Battles for the Ukraine 1941-1944
description: "Von Manstein: Battles for the Ukraine 1941-1944" is an Operational level simulation of land warfare in South Russia during the 1941-1944 period. The map covers the Ukraine from Stalingrad West, and parts of Poland and Rumania. Uses a single Ratio Combat Results table to determine outcomes of battle. Provides Air War points to be allocated. Units are essentially Division and up. Game contains 8 different scenarios (from 6 to 12 turns). Rules are about 5 pages (with specific additional items in the scenario) and 200 counters
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: Be the first player to place all of his magnetized sailors on board the ship.  Sailors being placed on the ship can either attract or repel each other.  If your sailor attracts then he is on board, if he repels then it's man overboard.

Be the first player to get rid of all your donuts by placing them on the soft rod on top of the temperamental donut-making machine before it explodes and sends everybody's donuts flying. If it does so on your turn, try to catch as many donuts as you can so you don't get stuck with them.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: The object of the game is for a player to score ten "triple plays" by filling 10 triangles on the center wheel with marbles. Players try to accomplish this task while the center wheel rotates.

Which came first--the beginning of construction on Notre Dame Cathedral, or the printing of the Gutenberg Bible? Test your historical perspective with Perspective. Two to eight players try to place event cards in chronological order in four categories: Ancient, Middle, Modern, and 20th Century. If you think one of the other players is "out of order", challenge them. Flip the question cards to reveal the dates on the back. If you're right, you get to give that player one of your cards. When all of your cards are played, you win! A fun way to impart historical knowledge and a frame of reference. Contains 100 basic set cards, 100 master set cards, board, custom die and rule sheet.

In Formula One Grand Prix Racing, the drivers' outcome is determined by his/her abilities of skill and judgment combined with elements of luck and fate. These same factors have been integrated into the Australian Formula One Grand Prix board game. Characteristics of actual Formula One race cars, tracks, teams, and sponsors have been included so that the game may be interesting as well as informative. You will surely experience the thrills and some disappointments that make Grand Prix racing so exciting!

Your mission is a deadly one, find the Laser Diamond in the City of Zinj.  If fear doesn't stop you - hippos, earthquakes and gray gorillas just might,  Can the expedition survive long enough to reach the city, then find the hidden diamond?  Set your life energy dial.  Then roll the dice to follow the jungle path.  Escape danger by spinning special spinners and rolling dice.  But watch out.  Life energy levels can get critically low.  Players may have to protect each other to survive.  In the City of Zinj, search for the diamond.  Leave no stones unturned.  The player who finds it wins the game.
family: Country: Democratic Republic of the Congo

description: Hades has taken over Mt. Olympus, and it's up to you to save the day.  Make your way to the top of the mountain with the help of your trusted winged stallion, Pegasus.  But be careful - a battle with a terrifying Titan or evil monster could send you down the mountain.  Be the first to reach the summit and restore Zeus to power, and you win.

Each of you will be rolling 15 poker dice in an attempt to form certain card-hand combinations in the first roll when scoring is highest.  If you are unsuccessful, take three more successive rolls, but score fewer points on each roll.  However, you can save any of the dice and roll fewer, if you decide it is to your advantage.  Jokers count against you and are removed from play whenever they appear.  If you are unsuccessful after 4 rolls, score 0.  If you are successful, receive the number of points given on the score sheet for each successive roll.

Avoid desert obstacles and outwit opponents as you move each of your road runners to a new cave.  The road runners must be moved from their starting cave, each to a separate gameboard cave.  The first player to find a new cave for each of his/her road runners wins the game.

Kitty, puppy, bunny and turtle are waiting for you at the Littlest Pet Shop.  You may be able to take one home if you can match its pet card with its matching color Pet Food or Pet Carrier card.  Once you have a match, spin to see if the shopkeeper will let you take that pet home.  First player to collect 3 pairs of matching cards and 1 pet wins.

This is a different game from the 2005 Littlest Pet Shop Game

Players choose one of four gargoyle characters. The gameboard is a four level tower. The object of the game is to reach the top level to rescue Elisa, the heroine, and defeat Xanatos, the villain. A player must battle any enemy pawns that share the same space as one's own gargoyle otherwise one may advance.

A player advances by following the random instructions of a spinner. A player does battle by choosing a tactic, each of which has a fifty-fifty chance of victory.

The game is based on the animated television series.

Tarzan takes to the trees in a game as tall as the legend.  Move your Tarzan from vine to vine, but don't land on a baboon and fall - Sabor may be waiting underneath.  Reach Tarzan's family first and win.

You and your opponents take turns placing multi-colored tiles on the board, arranging them so that their colors match the colors of neighboring tiles. If you use strategic planning, you can block out areas where you can score with a three-sided or four-sided match. When you think you've built up a large enough score, you can race to the finish by playing tiles that connect the two opposite sides of the board.

Colomino was published in the Ravensburger Casino Series.

TICK TACK MATH does for arithmetic what word games do for vocabulary. It is quickly learned and easily played, combining a mixture of strategy and luck. TICK TACK MATH is played on a board using numerical cards. Cards are dealt to both players and to the board. Players take turns making combinations of cards from their hands to match the board's cards. Colored chips are placed on board cards that have been matched. The first player to cover three in a row (across, down or diagonally) wins the game.TICK TACK MATH is challenging, suspenseful sic, thought-provoking FUN for children and ADULTS. It's an ideal parent-child game.

Stretch Call is a horse racing board game. The game is controlled by a special deck of 100 cards consisting of ten sets numbered from 1 to 10, 10 horses, a game board, wagering cards, play money, a grease pencil, and instructions.

All players are bettors, with all horses moved by the cards. The cards are drawn one per horse per round.  The cards are added and determine the horse's position behind a reference line drawn on the board. The reference line advances each round as the race progresses.

The goal of this game is to score the highest amount of points in an agreed period of time.

A Family Card Game for 2 Players. Components include 54 Cards including 27 Offense cards and 27 Defense cards, Football Token, Yard Marker, Instructions, Gameboard.

Each player draws 4 cards into their hand from their respective deck (person who is currently on offense takes 4 cards from the deck labeled offense, defensive player does the same but from the defensive deck)

Each player chooses a card, places faces down and when ready both reveal the cards and then resolve the play. Afterwards, each player draws a card from their respective deck so they will always have 4 cards at the start of a play.

The offensive player has up to 4 cards (or downs) to get a 1st down. Punting is controlled by cards so if the offensive player does not have a punt card, they basically are forced to go for it regardless of field position. This is not Strat-o-Matic, folks

After scoring or turnover on downs, players shuffle all cards back into their respective decks and then exchange decks

This abstract game is played on a board made of five concentric squares. Each player starts the game with 14 pieces split evenly in two opposite corners. Their objective is to bring four pieces on the four corners of the innermost square. Players can capture opposing pieces by surrounding them.
 This game is part of the Abacus wood box series.

Every player gets two trains. One rides from noth to south, and the other from south to north. The players draw a start-card. There are two city names on a start-card. Each player puts his/her passengers on the start locations. Then the destination-cards are drawn. There are two city names on a destination cards. A stopover and the destination.

On a player's turn, he/she moves all the train one space forward. Next the player throws the dice and moves the trains of which he/she has thrown the colors. If a train passes a station, a passenger can get in the train or change between trains. When a passenger arrives at the stopover or at his destination, the player receives a station-card as prove he/she stopped at the station.

The first player which brought his/her both passengers to the right stations wins the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
This game was designed by the French club, Vortigern, to be played by correspondence and was later picked up by Azure Wish Edition to be sold as a commercial boardgame.  It is a game for six players which combines diplomatic negotiation, warfare, and religious strife.  Each player represents one of the primary religions of the seventh century AD:  Western Christianity, Eastern Christianity (or the Monophysites), Islam, Zoroastrianism, Norse paganism and Shamanism.  As such, one has at one's disposal missionaries to convert others.  However, one also controls the temporal power behind these faiths--the Shamanist player controls the Avars, the Franks belong to the Western Christian player, and so on.  Conquering other territories greatly increases the effectiveness of one's missionaries.

There are also a great number of minor religions, which also have their adherents.

There are two maps--one spiritual (showing the penetration of the various religions) and one temporal (showing the spread of the various empires).

As in Diplomacy, one writes orders secretly (after a round of negotiation) and all orders are resolved simultaneously, with combat as appropriate.

The object  of the SOCIETY TODAY Game is to reach The Best of All Possible Worlds before any other player, using your knowledge about people and social institutions, your intelligence, and sheer luck to overcome the many obstacles that life—and your opponents—may put in your path.

A roll-and-move board and trivia game. Contents are:

8 player tokens
32 Refusal tokens
32 shot-in-the-dark cards
16 limbo cards
24 imponderable cards
timer
dice rules with 300 questions

The true/false questions are about "society today". Many are factual (such as Q40: During the early 1960s income differences between black and white males with some college increased (true)). Others are subjective (such as Q37: At the same job level, Catholics are more conservative than Protestants (false, apparently)). Gameplay is roll and move, following the instructions on the square you land on. Shot-in-the-dark, limbo and imponderable cards are similar to community chest in Monopoly - random events that may be good or bad. With shot-in-the-dark cards you have to convince a judge (another player) of the merit of one side of a social issue.

Players compete to uncover the 3-digit combination to the safe in this intriguing game of deduction. 24 clue cards—24 possible combinations. Is the combination Under 7  Over 10  Does the number begin with S, as in Six or Seven? You'll soon find out if you play Safecrack Mystery Combination Game.

Each player is a modern prospector buying mines and hoping to strike valuable ores, like silver or gold. On his turn, a player rolls three dice and then skillfully arranges them. Two dice form the Mine Number, which indicates where a strike occurs if the mine is already owned, or indicates the mine the player can buy if it is not owned. When a strike occurs, the third die indicates the type of ore found and, thus, the value of the strike. It pays to own several mines that lie adjacent to one another on the board as adjacent mines help to increase the value of a strike. (A strike can be worth as little as $5,000 or as much as $150,000!) The Object of the game is to be the first player to make a million dollars.

In The World Game of Economics each player is in control of a country's economy. This is one of the most important responsibilities facing a nation's leader. Everyone wants full employment, low inflation, economic growth and a high quality of life. As a player of the game, your challenge is to achieve these goals. The game may be played  as a game,  or it can be used to study current economic events. It is primarily designed for fun and requires no understanding of economics. The special training which economists have will not help them win. However, there is an economic principle behind each aspect of the game. Players who wish to learn more about economics may refer to the "Dictionary of Economic Terms" at the end of the rules.

The Race for Space is a serious look at the space technology race of the 50's and 60's.  There are three two-player scenarios, all of which are playable solitaire as well.  The game covers the period from 1955 to 1970 from the first attempts to orbit a satellite to the first manned landing on the moon.  Players develop hardware and systems through research, then conduct launches to test those systems.  Finally, they announce public launchings in an attempt to earn prestige points (victory is determined by prestige) and through prestige points to increase funding.  The game comes with 108 mounted but not die cut counters, 6 development displays, two launch displays, plus various charts, record sheets, and tables.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Think "Hungry Hungry Hippos" with extra stuff.

4 players' marbles are dumped into a hopper. Each player pushes a button as fast as they can to fill a channel with 5 marbles. They can take the marbles of their own color to put in their tray, which is cat-shaped. Then, dump the other marbles back into the hopper and keep pressing. Fill your tray with your own marbles (plus the "cat's eye") and win.

From the designer's description on Web-Grognard's (slightly edited):

Game Auction is a satirical look at the wargaming hobby and the games, people, and companies that make up the hobby. With tongue firmly planted in cheek, I have designed a game that simulates all the fun and craziness of a live wargame auction. Based on MODERN ART, the game adds event cards that may be played on yourself or on an opponent during the course of an auction (and includes such cards as "buying frenzy" where your emotions take over and you want to buy anything that has cardboard and dice, or the ever popular "Paranoid" where a player has come to the conclusion that a person is bidding against him not for the game but to out bid him--it has become personal). The game also adds condition cards that are played after a round of auctions is completed. It simulates that moment when you finally open the box that you've just purchased. With condition cards, you can find anything from a designer's autograph to mildew. The game also comes with a complete description of the companies and games that are up for sale. You will run into companies like EGO Games, "considered by many including themselves to produce the ultimate wargames, this company features some of the most famous personalities in the hobby. Renowned for their use (some would say abuse) of color, their games feature everything a wargame should have and a lot that isn't necessary. Their motto is 'If you can't understand our games, we are not surprised.'"

A game (primarily for preschoolers) that teaches children about Passover, Sukkos, Shavuos, and the High Holy Days. The game can be played on two different levels, for older and younger children. Options permit it to be a simplistic card-matching game, or a more advanced memory game with different options for playing.

boardgame: Around the Jewish Year: The Game That Takes "A Year" To Play!
description: A board game that takes its players on an informative journey through the Jewish calendar, replete with the holidays, festivals, and days of mourning. Merit cards, gift cards and puzzle pieces add to the excitement of this tour of the Jewish year.
The fast-paced game of card-flipping fun.  The object of the game is counting backwards.  Be the first one to get rid of all of your cards and you win.

In Find Furby, each furby has a unique silly sound or phrase all its own.  Find Furby will ask you to find a silly sound like "doobedoo" or a funny phrase like "party".  Can you find the correct furby to match with the furby sound.

College: The Game of Knowledge puts you to the test with questions in nine different subjects including History, Literature, Geography, Art, Music, General Science, Chemistry, Physics and Biology.  Choose your curriculum (liberal arts or science) and you are on your way to hours of fun.  First player to complete his curriculum by answering all required subjects is the winner.

boardgame: Doug Loser Proof Matching Transforming Game
description: Doug Funnie wants to be anything but average.  In this game, you can make him Loser Proof.  Find a matching pair of Doug's friends and put a Loser Proof card over part of Doug's picture.  After 10 matches, Doug's new picture is complete.  Now, he's Loser Proof and you're the winner.
Object is to cover the flowers on the card with butterflies.

If I tell you Leviathan is a cross between 'Go Fish' and a non-collectible 'Magic: the Gathering', you will instantly know pretty much everything you need to know about this game. Players take turns asking each other for cards matching the ones in their hands, each card being named after a particular fish (or fish-related being, such as Atlantean Priests or Mermaids). When a player manages to get a pair, they place it face-up on the table in front of them, just like in Go Fish. Once the cards hit the table, the Magic: the Gathering aspects come into play, as most of the fish have unique powers that can be used to aid you or annoy your opponents. There are also unique cards (with names like "Flying Dutchman" and "Offshore Oil Rig"), which have more esoteric powers and move from player to player as they get used. When one player runs out of cards in their hand, everyone scores a point for each pair they possess, and a new hand is dealt until every player has dealt once, at which point the final scores are tallied and a winner is declared. (Game time is thus dependant on number of players -- my guess of 90 minutes is a rough estimate of a 6-player game, which may not be at all accurate.)

In many ways, Leviathan epitomizes the "beer-and-pretzels" game -- in fact, it was designed in a coffee shop, out of a need for something to do while sitting in a coffee shop. The rules encourage a light ("jovially rude") atmosphere, and the writing style generally reflects this, although careful attention has been paid to the way the various cards interact, and to fix any possible contradictions or loopholes. (And the rule set has been edited to remove unnecessary rudeness in the author's writing style, although -- shh! -- an "impolite" version of the rules is rumored to exist, somewhere...)

Players are encouraged to devise new card powers and game variations, and as a bonus for players south of the equator, a special "Southern Hemisphere" option is given, which explains the way the Coriolis Effect changes the order of play.

The cat and the mice are placed on the gameboard. The yellow tokens are set aside. The blue token is used in case of a tie. Players can move their mouse by tapping the wheel to the left or right. As the player turns the wheel, the turntable rotates the mouse.
As Claude, the cat, begins to moving around the gameboard, all players at the same time quickly turns their mousemover wheels, trying to keep their mice away from Claude's mouth. If a mouse is caught by Claude's teeth, then that mouse has been catnipped! All play stops.

The owner of the mouse takes a yellow token and places the mouse back on its turntable. The game continues untill all of the yellow tokens have been collected. The player with the fewest tokens wins the game and is the Big Cheese!

In case of a tie, the players have a play-off round. The first player caught by Claude's teeth takes the blue token. The other player wins the game.

This is a four player action game. Each player controls two paddles, one advances the two garbage trucks that move on a turntable in the center of the game while the other shoots marbles (trash) into a chute positioned above the turntable. Objective is to dump your trash into the garbage trucks as they pass under your chute. One of the trucks, however, has a hole in it, meaning it can pass trash back into the player's bin.

The first player to get rid of all his marbles wins.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Swack is Ideal's wacky, new game that's loaded with action.  What happens when you take the cheese off the giant trap...just wait and see.  You might move ahead on the big cheese scoreboard or you might move back if the trap goes swack.

On a player's turn he or she can take up to 3 pieces of cheese from the pan. The player must take at least 1 piece of cheese from the pan. A player gains 3 points for each large piece of cheese and 1 point for each small piece of cheese taken. If the trap springs, the player springing the trap loses 10 points.
Players take turns removing cheese until someone springs the trap- then all of the cheese taken is replaced and the trap is reset.
When a player reaches the end of the scoring track- the game ends immediately.

Can you be the first to get your three monkeys to safety at the top of the banana tree?  Spin the spinner.  Your monkey might climb, or go to the top, or grab a ride on another monkey.  But don't spin that gorilla, cuz that grabby guy is just waiting to climb and knock you off the tree.  How high will he go?  Will he miss you or send you flying?  So start climbing but hang on tight, cuz this is Monkey Madness.
Hopper's coming, and Flik needs your help.  Save the ants by being the first to build a 3-D sparrow.  Spin to get the sparrow parts you need.  Finish first, and you win.
Be the first player to move your ducks completely around the crazy house without taking a swim in a bucket.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

Monopoly type game designed for alumni, students and friends of the University of Pittsburgh.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

Monopoly type game designed for Alumni, students and friends of the University of Pennsylvania.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description: Players try to score the best poker hand on every column and row of an 8x8 grid. One player has all the red cards of a standard deck; the other player has all the black. Players draw the top card off their respective stacks and place it on the grid until the board is full. Each row and column has one blocked-off spot which explains the game title. The higher hand in each row gets the points. Included with the board game is a CD-ROM version to play against your PC.

Based on the short-lived 1975 game show taped on location in Hawaii. The last contestant remaining moved to the Money Volcano. This was a wind tunnel in which the contestant had 15 seconds to grab as many paper slips as possible and place them in a pouch placed around their waist. Paper slips represented prizes, cash or $1 bills. When time expired, the host pulled out up to 10 paper slips (later 5 slips) from the pouch. There was a significant "push your luck" element as the player could stop at any time, but lost everything if the host drew a $1 bill. That particular risk is NOT recreated in this home game, and only peripherally follows the original gameplay from the show. (The show went through two versions of gameplay that can be seen in more detail here.)

The game is played in three rounds. The player who wins each round gets the greatest reward and the reward for finishing decreases the further behind a player finishes.

Round one: Players answer multiple choice trivia questions to advance on the gameboard. The person who gives the correct response acts as quizmaster for the following question. (With the show's original gameplay, pairs of contestants played a best two-out-of-three question test to progress to the next round.)

Round two: After hearing a list of items of a given category, the players must recall item from the list. Players alternate taking turns until one fails to call out a yet unnamed item. The other players advance when one fails to respond. (This somewhat mirrors the gameplay from both versions of the Round 2 rules.)

Round three: Players get the prize discs earned in the previous rounds. The player who earned the most in cash and prizes is the winner. (On the show, only the winner of Round 2 went to the "Money Volcano" for possible prizes.)

Now you can join in the exciting adventure of Pocahontas and Captain John Smith.  Begin by showing John Smith the way to the waterfall, where Pocahontas awaits.  Introduce them with a lucky turn of the dial.  Spin the pathfinder near Grandmother Willow.  Will she show you the way across?  Just ahead is Powhatan's hut, where John Smith is being held prisoner.  Turn the dial to set him free.  Collect tokens showing Native American and English ways of life.  You'll win by collecting the right tokens and returning John Smith to his ship.

Object is to act out your secret Charade so that your partner can guess it and score points for your team.  At the same time, you must keep your opponents from guessing and scoring points for their team.

To win, be the first player to collect the number of points you need to complete your personal "read me" profile.  How?  Move around the board through Sabrina's school and her house, collecting points from board spaces, the book cards, and by performing Sabrina's magic.

In this Roll and Move game, players pick up coconuts as they progress on the board. When players land on a square, the outcome is determined by comparing the picture of the monkey depicted in that square with the one on the monkey spinner.

The player who acquires the most coconuts from start to finish wins.
Spin the spinner and race through the beauty shop trying to collect all five of your fingernails to become the winner.

Triple Up is played with a mechanical contraption.  The device has a 4x4 grid at the top.  A metal rod goes through each column and a spring is mounted at the bottom of each rod.  A red ball rests upon each spring, and on each red ball is a 4-ball sequence, alternating in whites and blacks.

The players take turns to pull down a red ball and release it, launching the balls above into the grid, where some of the black-white balls remain, depending on the force used.  The goal is form a straight line (horizontal or diagonal) of 3 balls of a player's color.  The game ends when a player is able to do so, making him or her the winner.

This is a Roll and Move game inspired by Chutes and Ladders and played on a 3D board. When players fall on ladder spaces, they move up on the board but when they hit a tube, they slide way back.

The first fireman to rescue the kitten in the top window of the firehouse wins.

From the box:
A firehouse game with over two towering feet of fun!

Funny firemen climb up crazy ladders, and make silly sounds sliding down the poles. It's fun from the top to the bottom because you never know who's going to win. Just when you think you've made it to the top, you may slide to the bottom again! It's the game where it's just as much fun to start over as it is to win.

Comes with four funny firefighters that make their own silly sounds as they slide down the pole.

In this dexterity game, players grab worms using a hand-operated bird.

The player who places the most worm in his worm nest wins.

From the box: Grip that chirping bird's tail and wiggle its long, waving neck over the pile of worms.  How many of them can you grab with your bird's beak while your opponent and his early bird battle you for the same squirmy worm?

It's a fast and furious fruit fight where four hungry monkeys try to gobble up all the bananas they shake out of the banana tree.  Everybody, grab a monkey tail handle, then press it up and down and watch what happens.  The banana tree wobbles, the feeding tray wiggles and the bananas start to jiggle out of the treetop and down onto the tray.  Your monkey's mouth opens up wide to swallow all the little fruits that roll its way.  The monkey who has the most bananas when the banana tree is bare wins.

This game is a retheme of Hungry Hungry Hippos.

In this children's dexterity game, a player rotates a handle to spin bees past frogs which can be snapped closed by other players. Players keep the bees who made it past the frogs. The player with the most bees wins.

Shake, roll then match the lovable characters on the dice to those found on the game cards.  A special wipe-off surface lets you erase and use the cards again and again.

Match & Count (Side 1)
Roll all of the dice and set aside the character that appears the most. You have two more chances to re-roll the other dice to try to roll more of that character. You score points for how many of that character you rolled. After the first 6 rows are filled, players move to the bottom two rows, attempting to match the characters as shown, and score 5 points if they succeed.

Match Race (Side 2)
Each player gets one die (two if two people are playing). Choose a row or column on the card, and all players, at the same time, roll their die/dice and race to match all of the characters in that row/column, placing an X on that square as it is rolled.

Roll a Row (Side 2)
Roll all 5 dice, trying to match a sequence in any row or column on the grid. You have 2 chances to re-roll, and if you succeed, you may circle that row or column. The winner is the player with 5 rows/columns circled (they may overlap).

Once you start the game, the Crazy Crab jiggles, jerks, scoots and darts.  Crab claws from every player frantically grab for their pearls.  Just when you think you've got one, the crazy crab might knock it out of your claw.  The first player to get all of their colored pearls and then grab the crazy crab is the winner.

Try to reach infinity before your opponent by getting four marbles in a row.

Players have 8 marbles of their own colour, plus a joker. They take turns to place marbles in the bottom row of a 4x4 grid. marbles in the same column as the marble just placed move up a row, and if there was one in the 4th row it gets pushed out of the grid altogether (and is then available for the player to use again). After each turn, players score points for getting 4 marbles in a row, with extra points if their joker is involved. The winner is the first player to get 5 rows ahead of their opponent (to "reach infinity"), or to score two rows of 4 on the same turn.
A game where the player spins a spinner to move across the colorful playing board to get ready for Shabbos. When all the pre-Shabbos tasks have been completed, it's finally time to welcome Shabbos!

Are you ready for an interplanetary time-traveling treasure hunt?! Everyone loses things now and then, but what do we do when we FIND something? Now, you and your family can learn the halachos of hashavas aveidah, returning lost objects, while having loads of fun!

This memory game contains 20 durable playing pieces that feature pictures of Judaica and holiday-related objects. It consists of sets of interlocking pieces, and encourages players  to hone their knowledge of Jewish holidays and customs, by requiring them to piece together the objects that come together--ie. a Shofar and an apple for Rosh Hashanah.

The board game of Jewish values where players learn to take every opportunity to use money to do chesed, give tzedakah, and perform mitzvos, while encouraging others to do the same. Three challenging levels of questions.
A game of number knowledge, strategy and fun! Answer number questions, put down your piece, and fly across the board--if your opponent doesn't push you off first!

This card game contains beautiful, hand-drawn pictures of things used for Shabbat: Kiddush cup, challos, a havdalah set, and Shabbat candles. The object of the game is to get a match of four identical cards.

Based on the all-time bestsellers, The Kids Speak Series by Chaim Walder. This game teaches valuable lessons and gets players thinking about their lives and others. This game teaches integral values including respect for others, giving charity, doing good deeds, and guarding one's tongue.

Hurry around the game board looking for the prayers and blessings you need to complete your very own Siddur, in this charming game that teaches about the prayers, customs, laws, and blessings that make up the Siddur. The game provides opportunities for singing, telling stories, and recalling previously learned facts.

boardgame: Get Ready, Get Set, Good Shabbos!
description:
Players travel through town filling their bags with food. Mitzvos wait around every corner in this educational game.

From the publisher:
Preparing for Shabbos has never been so much fun! Players travel through town filling their bags with food, and their hearts with a love for Shabbos.

boardgame: Letter Getter: The Jewish Rummy Game
description: Traditional Rummy with a Hebrew twist! Form words and match up letter-tiles which come in Aleph Beis script, Rashi script, print, and gematria. Answer questions on the laws of Shabbos to collect prize-tokens and you'll find out just what your word's worth! A bilingual game on two levels.

Rauf & Runter is a small memory/guessing game for children who try to get around the palace garden over objects in the garden, without touching the ground with their feet.

Contents: 5 characters, 5 betting markers, 5 starting tiles and 21 tiles with an item on both the front and back. The items depicted on the 21 tiles can be 1 of 6 different objects, reaching from a mushroom (height 1), mole hill, bucket, dog house, horse and tree (height 6).

At first the 21 tiles are shuffled and placed randomly in a circle. Then each player selects a character and places the corresponding character and starting tile on one of the 21 tiles. He also takes the betting marker in his hand.

Players then take turns in clockwise order. When you correctly guess if the next tile is above, below or at the same height (by using the betting marker) than the one you're standing on, you can move one tile forward. You can guess up to three times correctly before your turn ends. As soon as you've guessed wrong, you are moved one tile backwards and your turn ends.

The player to have moved over all the tiles and returns to his starting tile wins the game.

Triumph is a checkers-like game for 2-4 players, played on a hexagonal board with triangular spaces and tetrahedron-shaped pieces.  The aim is to capture your opponents' pieces, which are removed from the board.  The winner is the last player with pieces remaining.

This junior edition of Letter Getter is specifically designed so that parents and children can learn Alef-Beis letters together. Game contains two sets each of print and script Alef-Beis letters and suggestions for many creative ways to play and learn.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description: This is a game of brachos (blessing) matching. Players must match coins depicting different foods, back-to-back with foods of the same brachah, creating towers, while racing against fellow players. Alternatively, players can arrange their coins in order of the brachos said on them.

With the 33 way-tiles and some extra pieces (like an oak, a castle, a bus-stop...) a path is build on the table that the players have to recognize. 
On their turn players have their pawn moved by describing which way they take and in which direction they're turning. Whoever reaches the end of the path first is the winner.

Small game which requires a huge portion of memorizing.

This game is new from Essen 2003. An import, the game rules provided come in many languages (including English) and the components are language-independent. Description: Bring the knight to your castle to get valuable knight's cards. Get three knight's cards first to win the game.

The five cards with the road are placed between the two players. At each end of the road a castle card is placed. Both players get 12 cards in one color. All the cards are numbered. Every round the players place a card face down on the table. At the same time the players turn around their cards. The player with the highest card wins the duel and moves the knight towards his/her castle. When the knight arrives at a castle, that player wins the knight.
If a player has three knight cards, he or she wins the game.

The dog of inspector Cluedo, Watson, has hidden a number of items in the school of detectives. The children of the inspector are trying to find the items to advance to junior detectives. This games is distributed in the Nordic countries.

The game seems to be a simplified version of "Clue Jr. The Case of the Hidden Toys."
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
"The battle of the Yellow Line". Kesselring defense on the Gothic Line. 
The protagonists assesments:
"This campaign is a hard row to hoe. It is the most difficult country in Europe, and yet we always get troops and equipment taken away from us for elsewhere. We have done all the fighting on a very narrow margin of relative strenghts".The battle of Rimini was "one of the hardest battles of Eighth Army. The fighting was comparable to El Alamein, Mareth and the Gustav Line (Cassino)". (Sir Oliver Leese)

The battle of Rimini "had been as bitter as El Alamein and Cassino". (Sir Richard McCreery)

"On the Apennines, south of San Marino, was fought the biggest battle in Italy: the names of Fano, Pesaro, Cattolica, Riccione and Rimini remain in the history of war." ( Beelitz and Heckel, German H.Q., Bellaria, Summer 1945)

"The battle of Rimini, as characterised by concentration of material in a confined area, will take its place in history as an example of a battle of attrition in the grand style.. For the first time the German troops could conduct defensive operations in a major battle as laid down in the German manual "Field Service Operations" (Truppen Fuhrung)" (Col. Horst Pretzell, German H.Q., Bellaria, Summer 1945)

"The battle of Rimini has been the biggest battle of materials fought in Italy" (Schäffer & Wöbbeking in Gen. Joachim Lemelsen '29.Division')

"The battle of the Apennines is a famous page in the military history of Germany". (Generalfeldmarschall Albert Kesselring, Commander Group of Armies 'C', South-West)

"The XV Army Group performed a successful undertaking that surely has never been exceeded.The Gothic Line was stormed by the American 5th Army and by the British 8th Army." - (Retroversion from Sir Harold Alexander's  "Memoirs")

"Victories, like the taking of Rome and the storming of the Gothic Line keep their name through the centuries." (Eric Linklater)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
In Baboon Ball, the game board is a kind of hockey pitch, but four monkeys wield clubs to score goals with a ball. Behind each monkey there is a hole. When the ball falls into that hole, the respective monkey loses a banana point; when all points are lost, that monkey is out.

The monkeys can be turned and pushed back and forth with a knob; they move in a rail-like track.

The objective of this Roll and Move game is to be the first player to exit the graveyard. The mummy and the skeleton may send players flying off the board if they land on their spaces. Players can turn the Dial-of-Doom to trigger surprises.

From the box
Welcome to Grave Danger, the game that features a gruesome graveyard packed with all kinds of strangely weird things.  Now that you're here, the first thing you'll want to do is get out.  that, my friend, is how you win.

Enjoy the fun and adventure of the Last Action Hero movie in this easy to play three-dimensional game.  Players travel from the real world to the movie world and back by using their magic movie tickets just like Danny does in the movie.  But, watch out for the villains.  Will Jack Slater be able to help you?  Players try to be the first to complete the 3 scenes of the Last Action Hero movie and get back to Danny's house.
A question and answer game that increases your knowledge about Jewish history and culture. An advanced version was published in 1978.

Skarney Kards contains a rule book and score pad for two card games and 30 variations written by the late, self-proclaimed "World's Foremost Game Authority" John Scarne. Skarney Kards plays much like a simplified version of Canasta in which discards are passed around the table instead of forming a discard pile. Skarney Gin is much like Rummy except that, as in Skarney Kards, discards are taken back if no opponent accepts them. Skarney Gin also allows players to meld runs of mixed suits but an initial lay must consist of exactly 3 sets of 3 cards each.

rules for Skarney Gin found as varient under bgg for Gin Rummy

The exciting group game where people write their quick reactions to words as they hear them and try to match other players.  Make the most matches and be first to use all of your make a match point chips.

Tough Luck is similar to Tripoley (aka Michigan Rummy) but with trick-taking instead of set collection. Each player antes on the 8 money card spots on the game mat plus the Bonus Pot. Players who win cards in a trick that match the spots on the mat get to collect the chips on that spot UNLESS that trick also contains a Tough Luck deuce. In that case, the chips stay on the mat until the next round when that card will be worth even more chips. The Bonus Pot goes to the player who takes the most tricks in a round UNLESS there is a tie in which case the bonus pot is carried over into the next round too.

For 2 to 6 players Ages 8 and up.

Based on the long running (1960-65) game show, players try to make it past a 70-square playing field in a maximum of 15 moves. The player moves from 1-10 spaces, landing on questions and spots such as Safety, Bonus (move ahead by up to 10 more spaces), and Penalty (move back by up to 10 spaces); missing a question puts the player back at the first spot (or the last Safety reached)

If the player makes it to the end of the board, s/he wins a Key which can be used to unlock prizes like a pen, a gallon of ice cream, a typewriter, or a movie camera. Once all seven Keys have been won, the game ends.

Managing Your Money appears to be a long out of print promotional game released by the Cuna Mutual Insurance Society. The object of the game is to be the first person to repay the 2,000 dollar credit union loan and collect 2,000 dollars in their savings account. After gaining a vocation card, the players move clockwise around a board.

The spaces are outlined with various bonuses and penalties -"Pension Payment: collect 50 dollars" and "Extended Sickness: do not collect salary when passing Credit Union office" and so on. There are two smaller separate tracks - Hard Times and Goodtimes, that the player can access if they land in the right spot. Along these tracks, the player can enjoy extra benefits or suffer additional major blows to their pocketbook.
Join the Young Maccabees and experience the thrills and miracles on their quest to liberate Jerusalem.

First and foremost the object of the game is to have some good old-fashioned fun swapping stories.  Secondly, the object is to correctly guess whether your opponent is telling a tall tale or a total truth or to bluff your opponent with a story of your own.

Players compete to collect word tiles to complete a phrase in German. Chatter has two aims: first to produce a winner (the player who collects the most word-tiles) and second, to allow the players to understand and remember foreign words and phrases.

The contents of this game are: 40 illustrated phrase-cards, 4 sets of color coded word tiles, 6 phrase cards and word tile holders, 1 game bag, 1 translation master key and 1 set of rules.
designers: Drakes, Jarvis, Walsh, and Gluck, Ltd.

description: Players must correctly answer questions to move their piece around the board.  Once a player returns to their home corner, they move to the center telephone dial where they must correctly answer seven questions to win the game.

A fast moving game based on the supersonic jetliner. The game places you in the position of a Concorde fleet manager, testing your commercial skill - and providing you with hours of fun.
As a fleet manager you collect passengers, organize aircrew, buy fuel at the right price and decide when is the best time to take off. You decide whether to fly at subsonic or supersonic speed and which route you will take to reach your destination.
The object of the game is to have earned the most money when the last Clearance Card is used, and once all Concordes have reached their final destinations.
Be the first player to get rid of all your cards.  The game consists of 2 dice and 51 playing cards.  2 for each even number combination of the dice and 3 for each odd combination of the dice.

Pocket Ungame - Couples is another expansion for "The Ungame" that can be used as part of the original board game or by itself as a standalone talking game. Like other Ungame expansions, this one comes with 2 decks. Deck one contains light questions meant as ice breakers and deck 2 contains questions meant to spark deeper conversations.

In Pocket Ungame - Couples Version, players are encouraged to share their opinions, feelings, and beliefs through the asking of simple questions.  Questions come from card prompts; there is a 'light-hearted' deck and a more serious deck of questions.  Ideal as an ice-breaker as it is fundamentally non-competitive.

Contains 140 cards.

From the Publisher:
Examples of Pocket Ungame “Couples” Version:

What quality do you like best in your mate?
What is something you really like about your relationship?
What adjustments must be made when a couple has children?
What emotion is most likely to interfere with your relationship?

boardgame: The Lost World Hunt...and Be Hunted Card Game
description: Are you a human or a dinosaur?  If you're a human, you're hunting for Triceratops, T-Rex and the other prehistoric giants.  Dinos are after the likes of Dieter, Sarah and their machines.  So draw a card - and start hunting.  If you bag the prey you're after, you can stop now or you can keep on hunting.  Will you play it safe, or risk it all and go for more?  Remember, in the Lost World, you have to take chances.

The Not-So-Good Guys are hiding out in the cards in your hand.  Be the first player to capture one of each of the Not-So-Good Guys out of your (or an opponent's) hand and play it on the table in front of you, and you win - maybe.

3,448 questions and answers for teens and adults.  Ideal for home or compact for travel.  Family Trivia Game complete on its own or a great add-on to other trivia games.
Categories:
1 Rock 'N' Roll Video
2 Jock Talk
3 Facts As Figures
4 Useless Information
5 In So Many Words
6 Box Office
7 Birds And Bees And Trees
8 People And Places
Collect the most points.  Each card counts 5, 10 or 15 points.  Players match marbles in the Risky Roll box to patterns on the cards.  A correct match wins the card and thus the points.

boardgame: Mars and Venus in Love Question Card Game
description: A pack of provocative questions that will keep you guessing what men and women are thinking.  Score points by successfully guessing other players' answers.  It's thought-provoking fun for whenever friends get together.  Or use as additional questions with your Men Are from Mars, Women Are from Venus Game.

The sensational game for unleashing the adventurous spirit within you.  Ideal for sparking up a romance.  The perfect gift for any occasion or that special intimate evening of passion.  The object of Fore-Playing Cards is to tease and please each other with favors and to see how long you can last.  Cards are used to play either Blackjack or Poker.

The object of the game is to have fun while you discover just how well you really know your mate.  You and your mate pit yourselves as a team against other couples and win points by answering questions about yourselves and your relationship.

boardgame: Love, Sex and Romance Trivia Card Set
description:
This trivia card set is designed to be played as a stand alone game, or as a companion to such related games as Avalon Hill's Game of Trivia and Selchow and Righter's Trivial Pursuit.  Contains 6 categories:
1 Passionate Pages
2 Sizzling Screen
3 Love Themes
4 Simply Shocking

6 Potpourri

Color coded cards for use with the most popular trivia game boards.  Contains 2,472 interesting and hilarious questions and answers from the world of adult humor.Categories are:

6,000 new trivia questions, 1,000 cards in 6 favorite categories:

Cards are color-coded for use with the Trivial Pursuit Master Game Board.

boardgame: Rich and Famous Trivia Card Set
description:
This trivia card set is designed to be played as a stand alone game, or as a companion to such related games as Avalon Hill's Game of Trivia and Selchow and Righter's Trivial Pursuit.  Contains six categories:
1 Jet Set
2 Places and Palaces

4 Baubles and Bangles

Each player starts with 10 points.  The game features cards with questions/topics like "Yes" in any foreign language, countries of the British Empire, fads, and holes.  The first player draws a card and answers the question or says an item in that category. Then the second player answers the same question. When a player gives an incorrect, duplicate, or unacceptable answer, that player loses one point. The second player draws a card and a new round starts.If a player has lost all his/her 10 points, the game ends. The player with the most points left, wins the game.

The object of the game is to be the first player to get five numbers in a row either vertically, horizontally or diagonally.  The pop-o-matic dice roller shows what number and letter (B-I-N-G-O) to depress on your bingo board.

Climb the intelligence ladder and compare your word IQ with your friends.  Each of you begins rated equally ignorant, and there's plenty of room for improvement. You'll be competing to progress through the ratings of ignorant, average and smart attempting to become the game's Genius. Players climb the intelligence ladder by correctly answering Quiz Cards and comparing their answers to the game's infallible answer man, Quizzer. It tells you if you're wrong and how many spaces to move on the board.

Take this travel-size movie mania for kids with you wherever you go to entertain your junior traveling companions.  Players take turns drawing cards containing movie trivia from children's movies.  The player who answers the question correctly keeps the card.  The first player to possess three cards from both categories wins.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description: How good is your memory?  Good enough to remember 20 pictures if they flash briefly on the screen?  What about 34 pictures? Or 41?  What if they flash at a rate of 10 per second?  Could you pick them out of a crowd?  And could you make your opponents think they saw pictures that never really appeared?  You'll need all of these skills to play Flash Match, the VCR game that tests your ability to concentrate, remember and bluff.

The game is based on the lives of Neanderthals. In the game teams must find all of the items they need to survive. Among these items are food, water and weapons. The hunt must be performed in teams of two. The game becomes tricky when one source of water or food that is normally available is dried up. Teams must quickly determine an alternate source.

The best way to win the game is to find and secure water and food first. Drying up resources will force the their team to have to go further out to secure the same basics. This will then allow you time to gather the minor items, while they are still searching for basic resources.

Players try to maneuver their flies along the spider web to safety; but from time to time, the spider will descend, plucking some of the flies into his arms. Then it takes a special dice roll to rescue your flies and begin again.

Source: Phil Vettel, Chicago Tribune

From the box:
As you race to get off the web, this spider hangs by a thread, swaying back and forth over you.  Then slowly but surely, with one little jiggle, it starts to inch down, then another little jiggle, lower and lower, then another jiggle, closer and closer to the web, until Swoosh.  It pounces and snatches up any little flies underfoot.  The only way to safety is to get all your flies off the web, before it strikes again.

Players try to complete a trip around the circular track by successfully reading tongue twisters while competing against the ticking timer.  Opponents can secretly set the timer for random periods so players never know how much time remains.

Players move up the mountain trying to stay on the main path of Success.  As they climb upward they may stray onto the paths of Doubts, Delays and Setbacks.  During the course of the game, the players are invited to share their own real life experiences, and describe how they overcame their problems through possibility thinking.

boardgame: Butt-Ugly Martians: The Race to Rescue Dog
description:
The famous martian hunter, Stoat Muldoon, has captured Dog and is holding him captive deep within his secret silo.  Without Dog, the Butt Ugly Martians can't transmit their phony battle report to the old swagtar, Emperor Bog.  If Bog doesn't receive his update in time, he'll take matters into his own hands, and conquer earth.  It's up to you to help the hyperkinetic Butt Ugly Martians get into butt kickin' mode and rescue Dog.  It's easy to get lost in Muldoon's maze, so follow the arrows and try to stay on track.  The first person to race through the silo and reach Dog wins.

Each turn the player roll the blue die and moves his/her pawn in the direction of the arrows. If the space where you land is a Butt-Ugly Martian space or aStoat Muldoon space, draw a corresponding card and immediately do what is says. Keep going. Landing on a room spaces ends your turn.
When you land on a Emperor Bog Dr Damage space, roll the black Emperor Bog die. If Emperor Bog Dr Damaged is rolled, move him one space closer towards earth.
The first player to reach Dog wins the game. When Emperor Bog reaches the earth, before Dog is rescued, the game is lost.

You just hit the Whomping Willow with your flying car and the Willow goes wild.  Can you save your luggage without getting whomped?

The game has three types of play: Time Trial, Collection, and Free-for-All.

- In the Time Trial, each player competes individually to use their flying cars to remove luggage from the tree, racing against the game's internal clock. Any number of players can take part in the Time Trial.
- Collection is for 2 to 4 players. Everyone plays simultaneously, racing to be the first player to remove one of each type of luggage (suitcase, books, owl) from the tree.
- Free-for-All, also for 2 to 4 players, challenges competitors to collect the most pieces of luggage from the tree.

The game has two modes; easy and advanced. In advanced, the tree limbs spin faster and less predictably.

The Chipmunks Go Hollywood is an exciting new board game where players take the roles of the 3 chipmunks and their girl friends, the chipettes.  If you can avoid the obstacles and be the first to get back to David Seville with his new Hollywood outfit, you'll be the winner.

David Seville's asleep! Now the Chipmunks and The Chipettes can sneak out and get him a surprise birthday gift from the Hollywood Gift Shop. The First one back home with a gift wins the game.

The object of the game is to be the first player to get back to the Start Space with a Gift picked up from the gift shop.

Gone with the Wind the Game consists of eight separate board, card and trivia games: Gone with the Wind trivia game, Atlanta, Carpetbagger, Frankly My Dear, Pay or Play, Rhett, Scarlett and Tara.

This particular game is a trivia board-game with a small twist. In addition to drawing cards and answering questions in your quest to win, this game includes two complete decks of playing cards. These form the 104 question cards, but can be used as regular playing cards as well. Instead of spades, hearts, clubs and diamonds, we have Scarletts, Rhetts, Mammies and Ashleys. There appears to be no correlation between the picture on the card, the subject matter of the questions, or the suit, but I'm sure I'm just not looking hard enough. The pictures are repeated on some cards, and not with regularity, so there must be something going on. Playing card collectors will like these decks.
In addition to the game itself, there are also rules for playing other games with only the cards, including variations on rummy and go fish, as well as other games that make use of the trivia questions.

The two large decks of question cards can also be used as regular playing cards. Standard suits have been replaced by Scarletts, Rhetts, Mammies and Ashleys. The art depicted on the cards is independent from questions. The rules for card games include variations on Rummy and Go Fish.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In this Roll and Move game, players try to be the first to reach the gold mine at the top of the mountain. However, as they progress, prospectors can be randomly sent back down by the Rattler! mechanical device.

From the back of the box:

It's a thrilling journey to the Lost Gold Mine in this suspense-filled game.  Move your prospector up the mountain trail, but beware.  There's a rattler inside the mountain, and when you hear the hiss, you'll know he's ready to strike.  If the rattler strikes through the door, you'll have to go back to start.  The first player to get his prospector to the Lost Gold Mine at the top of the mountain wins.

boardgame: Sesame Street Great Big Parade Game
description: Why, it's Sesame Street Great Big Parade Game. Enjoy all the excitement of a parade with a marching band and lots of fun balloons. And have a color-matching race game too. Spin the spinner and move along the path. Whenever you make a color match, give away one of your balloons to Big Bird, Cookie Monster or any other Sesame Street friend. Can you give away all your balloons and get to the end of the parade first?

Players compete to advance their tokens through the various points of achievement pictured throughout the game.  To win, a player must be the first to advance all of his tokens into the Success circle.  Patents are acquired and royalties are thereafter collected.  Special privileges and penalties are encountered during play, which enliven and enhance interest in the game.

Can you and your children find Little Bear and his friends? The game is simple: just spin the spinner, then pick up a Colorforms piece. The first player to collect Little Bear and all his friends wins the game.

Join in the fun as you travel around the neighborhood matching colors and collecting photos of your friends Barney and Baby Bop.  you'll even take photos of one another with Barney's super camera spinner.  If you're the first player to collect one each of all five sights, you win.

The object of the game is to move a stand-up playing piece along the pathway and take five pictures of Barney. Movement is determined by a spinner with a player moving forward to the color indicated by the result. The spinner is a clever representation of a camera. Players draw one or two cards when landing on designated areas. Some cards require a player to take a "pretend" picture of another contestant with the camera/spinner while that kid makes a certain facial expression. The Rainbow Picture cards are drawn randomly as player land on spaces that reward a lucky spin. Each player tries to assemble a complete set of five different Barney poses and win the game.

(description stolen from Pete Belli's review: http://www.boardgamegeek.com/thread/496407/i-love-you-you-love-me-were-a-happy-family-)

The first player to complete 5 laps around the track wins.  Collect as many of the virtue cards as you can in order to successfully pass Challenge spaces around the track.  Collect equipment cards to avoid track hazards that would send you back to the pit stop.  The gold faith cards are wild cards and may be used in place of equipment or virtue cards.

A sharp eye and some fast sorting fingers are all that you'll need in this fun, frantic card game.  Roll the colored die, flip a card and try to be the first player to find a matching object among your cards.  Whoever collects the most cards wins.

Ages 6 and up

Card Cubes the rolling card game includes 10 card cubes, cube shaker and point chips.  Game has 6 different games available:
1 San Francisco Cubes
2 Poker Cubes
3 Rolling Rummy
4 Queens
5 Royal Cubes
6 10 Cube Mexican Stand-off

The object of Sudz is to be the first player to get rid of all your cards while helping to build a soap opera storyline.  It is a game of speed in which all players play at the same time, racing to play cards to the center stack.

Tee Off, swing, stroke and putt your way through 18 exciting holes.  A golf dice game for those who can't get on the course.

The game comes with a set of 5 dice, a golf ball which doubles at the dice holder and shaker, and a set of score cards. Different dice are used for different shots - the red one to tee off, the green one to put, the yellow one from the fairway, the blue die for your approach shot and the white die for a second approach shot. Play is purely mechanical - you roll the die necessary as a result of the previous shot, with no scope for player judgement or skill.

boardgame: Ancient Empires: A Simulation of the Wars of Antiquity
description:
This is a different game from the Steven Mathers' Ancient Empires that's featured on this site.

This is a tabletop wargame using miniatures, which don't come with the actual game itself. It is based around the battles of Julius Caesar e.g. the Roman Civil war, the conquest of Gaul and so forth.
Contents of game box include: Gold Hot-Stamped leather ring binder, 100 page rule book, die-cut counters and color coordinated reference charts.

Expanded by:

    Trojan Wars
    Alexander
    The Successors
    Caesar
