description:
Welcome to the witching hour! Another 60 minutes of spell-binding interactive entertainment with Anne de Chantraine. Nightmare III- the Sequel to The Video Board Game Nightmare that turns a maiden into a monster!
This is hot-tempered HI-FI horror in a VHS cassette, with new sets of Time and fate cards, twice the dice and three times the fun.
We are on a witch hunt and ugly has a new name. Anne de Chantraine.
Anne de Chantraine was 17 years-old when convicted of witchcraft and burn alive at the stake. She was, naturally, innocent, hence the players response of "Mea Culpa Anne". Latin for "I am culpable".
Simply insert the new cassette, press play and meet the girl of your dreams in your worst Nightmare.
Anne de Chantraine. Witch. Nightmare. "Time to turn up the heat!"

Once bitten, twice shy! Take a turn for the worst and sink your fangs into another 60 minutes of HI-FI fright with The Blood Countess Elizabeth Bathory! - the Sequel to The Video Board GameNightmare that's literally shocking the world!
The Blood Countess Elizabeth Bathory is one of the most murderous Vampires in the history of horror. Born in Hungary in 1560, Bathory killed more than 650 young women, and took early morning baths in their blood in a quest for eternal beauty. Imprisoned in her own castle for her perverse crimes, she died at 64. Or did she?
Now, it's not just a race against time but a game of sheer survival as this bat out of hell bares her teeth in the most challenging sequel in the Nightmare series. No game ever plays the same twice!
Press Play and scream the night away with new sets of Time and Fate cards plus additional Black Rose cards and an interactive Book of Rules.
Countess Elizabeth Bathory. Vampire. Nightmare IV... to love and perish forever!

OBJECT OF THE GAME:
Be the first to call out "HIGH", "LOW", "HIGH/LOW", or "ZIPPY" after totaling the target number(s) shown on the green dice, with the numbers shown on the black dice using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

THE DICE:
There are three large green dice, two of which have the numbers 1,2,3,4,5,6 and one with the numbers 4,5,6,7,8,9. The die with the numbers 4-9 is used for the advanced game.

The six smaller black dice have the standard numbers 2-6, but the remaining side can be used either as a 1 or a 7, depending on the player's choice.

GAME PLAY:
The 4-9 large die is set aside. All players play at the same time, taking turns as to who will be the 'roller'. The other eight dice (2 large and 6 small) are simultaneously rolled in full view of all the players, and must not be touched by ANY player.

The two Large Dice form a 2-digit number, which can either be high or low. For example, if the large dice show a 3 & 6, the low number would be 36 and the high number would be 63. Players study the dice and quickly call "HIGH", "LOW", "HIGH/LOW" or "ZIPPY" AFTER they have found a way of totaling either of the two numbers formed by the large green dcie with the small black ones using addition, subtration, multiplication and division.

The first person to call and get a correct result gets 1 chip, unless they call HIGH/LOW and show both answers - then they get 2 chips. You lose the same amount of chips if you are incorrect. If you call ZIPPY (which means no possible way to reach the target values) you get a chip from the bank unless someone challenges you. Then, whomever is correct pays the other.

The advanced version uses the 4-9 die in place of one of the other large dice for more challenging possible results.

UK players will see this as a dice driven version of the numbers quiz from the TV show Countdown.

This book offers a large number of variations on many classic board and card games, as well as listing some new games that can be played with components from those games.

While it is comparable to Sid Sackson's well known gaming book (A Gamut of Games) in some respects, New Rules for Classic Games also explains how and why certain variations work. It also provides some guidelines on creating new variations for existing games, including tips on how to change the number of players or to handicap one side effectively. Because of this, New Rules for Classic Games is recommended for people interested in game design.

Rules are given for the induction game of Eleusis, and several variations on Sid Sackson's classic party game Haggle are described.

Variations are also provided for many classic board games, including:

    Monopoly
    Trivial Pursuit: Genus Edition
    Chinese Checkers
    Boggle
    Mastermind
    Hearts
    Scrabble
    Chess
    Checkers
    Backgammon
    Risk
    Pente
    Bridge
    Poker
    Go

description: Waterloo is vaguely related to Stratego, in that each player moves pieces representing soldiers around the board, and that each piece has a value which can only be seen by the owner. However, this is more of a deduction game - when soldiers end up on the same space, instead of simply comparing values a complicated bidding process takes place which provides the players with information, which they have to use to guess the opposing piece's value.
Players take turns placing 3 different sizes of bears onto a balance beam (based on the roll of the dice). The player who wins is the first player to balance it perfectly.

Trail of the Brotherhood is an adventure game with a deductive element set in the world of H.P. Lovecraft in the 1920s as the Investigative player teams work as a group to discover the Old One the Sorcerer players is working to summon. In a race against time the Investigators will span the globe seeking to uncover clues, attacking cults that have joined The Brotherhood, and working to obtain the keystone magical item required for the final ceremony.  Will they succeed in this desperate quest, or will the Sorcerer player prevail, joining cults together to create the ritual’s power while wielding the required magic item to open the gate and wake the Old One they worship from slumber?  
 Trail of the Brotherhood is a game for 2 – 5 players designed by Richard Launius and published by EOS.

boardgame: Killer Bunnies and the Quest for the Magic Carrot: GREEN Booster
description:
We don't want to split "hares", but we have no choice with our new Half Color Bunnies. Featuring the weird world of the Zodiac, the Killer Bunnies Green Booster Deck adds 55 cards to your existing set, plus a twelve-sided Zodiac die!

Other cards include: Snowball which will send a chill down your spine, Fingercuffs to promote teamwork, Leif Carrotson to send your bunnies on a journey around The Bunny Circle, Zodiac Terminator (he'll be back!), and Albino Baby 5000 the world's most powerful radiation protector (and sun tan lotion).

Contents: 55 Large Cards, 12 Small Cards, A Twelve-sided Zodiac Die, and Instruction Book

Expands:

    Killer Bunnies and the Quest for the Magic Carrot

description:
Mayhem is an expansion for Vapor's Gambit. This expansion includes 6 expansion boards and 6 chits. There are additional turns and straight aways as well as a 4 way intersection and a narrow (2 spaces wide) straight away. Of the chits, there are more ramps and the addition of 2 damage repair chits.

This expansion allows you to build larger and more elaborate tracks.

boardgame: 1 on 1 Adventure Gamebooks: The King Takes a Dare
description:
Midnight in the city of New York. It's raining. Three miles away from each other, two men make exactly the same decision at exactly the same time.

One is Daredevil, the red-costumed fighter for justice, who uses his uncanny senses to fight evil where he finds it.

The other is Kingpin, the pinstripe-clad warrior, who has followed his own code of ethics while fighting and clawing his way to the top of a criminal empire more powerful than many small nations.

Each has decided to eliminate the other in a duel to the death on the streets of New York.

One player is Daredevil. The other, Kingpin. Each has his own book of scenarios and choices in this 1 on 1 Adventure Gamebooks episode of the Marvel Super Heroes Adventure series.

boardgame: 1 on 1 Adventure Gamebooks: The Amber Sword of World's End
description:
The game consists of two books, one for each player. One becomes Garth, a monk, Master of the North Wind. The other is his rival Uthrac, a powerful warrior. The two of you have been sent on a quest to the perilous place known as World's End to try to gain possession of the magical Amber Sword.

Race to be the first to win the magical and powerful Amber Sword.

An easy, diceless combat system simulates battle.

Part of the series of 1 on 1 Adventure Gamebooks.

"Heartpounding high adventure cleverly designed as cards.

Whiteouts, Avalanche, Frostbite, The Yeti,..... you'll face them all on your climb to The Summit."

Game includes Meter, Camp, Shortcut, Summit, Gear, Danger,  Avalanche, and Yeti cards.

boardgame: Super Mario Bros. "The Great Ladder Race Game"
description:
Players guide Mario and Luigi as they race down their ladders. The first one to reach the bottom of the ladder is the winner!

The game comes with a blue platform with a vertical tower that holds the two yellow ladders. The ladders spin on the vertical tower. Mario and Luigi have hooks on the backs of their heads and legs so that they can latch on to the ladder rungs. The object is to rotate the ladders back and forth to make Mario and Luigi climb down the ladder.
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
From the publisher:

Play the detective card game to put the Jigsaw together -- and then have a picture mystery case to solve. Each case has 40 clues and is 8 x 14 inches. The sealed envelope contains the Solutions, and a secret coded message for crime fighters everywhere. Lots of play value!

Rules included for a group co-op game for 12 to 36 players, using all four cases together.

Contents:
36 action cards
4 jigsaw mystery cases
solutions
rules

From the publisher:

Sixteen felt pieces that when put together make a train of zoo animals. The body parts are the same size so a child can create some new kinds of funny animals! If a friend comes over or if it's a Birthday Party, rules are included for using the puzzle as a Group Co-op Game.

The Game of Bare-Fanged Diplomacy.
True or False: Diplomats at the U.N. are dedicated to lofty causes like peace and world brotherhood.

If you answered 'True' you need this game. We'll cure that hopeless idealism - with riotous good fun!

If you said 'False', you want this game - where world-class brinksmanship is like a good ol' fashioned two-fisted domestic squabble. You're pretty good at that, right?

International Insult is today's new fast-playing, easy-to-learn, super-strategy, endless variation, tons o' fun card game!

You're a diplomat at the summit on Global Goodwill and Endangered Polyesters. Spies and saboteurs abound. Your goal: achieve lasting fame by elbowing past every rival from the "Vestibule of the Meek" all the way to the coveted "Mock Throne". You gain by insulting diplomats from other lands. War could result. True-to-life diplomacy ain't pretty - but 1,000 insults inside make it easy!

It's time to cut thru the substance and get right down to the froth. Time for a tempest in a teapot. It's time for International Insult.

Ages 10 to adult. 3-6 players.

Contents:

    90 Insult Cards (6 different nationality suits, each with 15 insult topics)
    Three types of wild cards ('kindling'): 6 Saboteur, 6 Spy, 12 Rude Waiter, and 6 This Means War
    6 National I.E. nameplates
    Play Board
    6 Color-coded Movers
    6 six-sided Dice
    Rules

ZByte: hightech playing cards

ZByte is a modification of a normal 52 card deck that replaces the standard suits with logic gates:  and, or, byte, and not.  This not only changes the form, but it also changes the function of the cards.  The symbols allow the cards to interact in new and different ways.  The number cards also display the bit value and binary representation on the face of the card.

Included with the cards is a booklet outlining the functions of the gates as well as games based on the applications of those gates.

From the back of the box:

ZByte is a revolutionary new design in playing cards based on computers - the first logical change in 500 years!

Create and play a new generation of games for the twenty-first century.

Or play all your favorite traditional games, such as Solitaire, Black Jack, Poker, and Gin Rummy.

This 52-card deck contains the standard number cards, aces, jacks, queens, kings, but  with four special suits, and two robot "IF" jokers.

Enclosed 32-page book has rules for eleven new games and instructions for ZByte game designers.

From the White Mask Productions website:

Tokar's Arcana is a simple, yet in-depth game of strategy and warfare. For the most part, it plays very similar to Chess. Each player has 8 common units and 8 special units, each with their own unique abilities. The object of the game? To capture the other player's general, of course. However, if you are playing a worthy opponent, this will not be an easy task...

From the MDG website:

At 5:30 AM, April 9, 1917, the artillery of British First Army unleashed the greatest barrage to that date. Half an hour later, 48,000 Canadian soldiers left their trenches to assault the impregnable Vimy Ridge. The French and the British had previously tried to capture the ridge but to no avail. On this battlefield the Canadians were to prove themselves. At 2:00 PM the Canadian troops held the ridge.

Vimy Ridge is a simulation of the Canadian attack. The game covers the battle from the initial barrage to the final objectives reached by 1st Division.

Vimy Ridge is the re-release of the game Vimy Ridge: 1917 formerly published by Pacific Rim. There are minimal changes in the rules but the graphics have been substantially changed.

Abstract game in which players try to form a pattern on the playing board. In the first phase of the game pieces are placed on the game. In the second phase pieces are moved around the board; Opposing pieces can be removed from the board in a rock-papers-scissors fashion.
A dinosaur trivia card game, based on the popular and award winning BBC series "Walking with Dinosaurs".  Includes 70 large cards and rules.  For players Ages 7 and up.

Calling all budding doctors and nurses. Anatomix is a fun way to build a body on your playing board. Choose from the skeleton, nerves, muscles and organs, the object is to be the first to complete your body. For younger players simply flick the spinner and take or trade body bits. For older players a multiple-choice question needs to be answered to gain, swap or take a body part. Play and learn more about your anatomy in this fabulous resource.

Learn and improve your knowledge of the body in this biology based game. With 2 different levels perfect for 5+ and 12+ players, this game is great to play with the whole family. This game is made from recycled materials. All questions are in English, French and Spanish.

Players    2 - 4 players or teams,
Ages    5 - 105,
Contains    48 Puzzle pieces, 4 player boards 84 question cards, 1 spinner, 1 rule booklet.,

CONTENTS

1 Codswallop Console
258 Codswallop description cards
6 Joker Cards
50 Wallop tiddlywinks
6 Yes/No Vote Cards

Object:

Simply put down description cards on the playing console, in turns, across a row or down a column to build a profile of almost anything! But beware of bluffing, because you may be "Codswalloped" and called on to name your object.

When 3 cards have been played on a line, shout the word they described and claim the points.

Music Boxx is a memory game to help teach players how to read music.  Cards have bass and treble clefs, and players attempt to match these with their playing boards appropriate notes.  The game may be adjusted in difficulty to accommodate growing abilities. Ages 5 and up
A unique deck of cards designed to teach children musical concepts using snaps and pairs.  Includes a self-checking guide.  Ages 5 and up
family: Series: Educational Snap and Pairs (Green Board Game Co)

description: Walking with beasts is a safari style game set in prehistoric times.  Each player controls a group of prehistoric animals that battle opponents.  Inspired by the BBC television series.  Ages 7 and up

Walking with Dinosaurs is a game based on the award winning BBC series of the same name. Players Ages 7 and up race around the board attempting to evade the T-Rex and other evolutionary casualties so that they may evolve and survive the Late Cretaceous period.

Box back blurb: "Includes a fast, fun version for ages 5+!"
Created for the movie of same name directed by Robert Altman. A commercial version of the game is available in Brazil, translated as "Quinteto".

boardgame: Deckmatch: The Unofficial Quake 3 Card Game
description: This is a do-it-yourself card game based on the computer game, Quake 3 Arena.  It can be played with an ordinary deck of cards, but works best if you have a way to create cards with the correct graphics (weapons) on them.  To find the rules, try doing an internet search for 'quake deckmatch'.  The game is a fast paced, turn-based simulation of a first person shooter, where the objective is to kill (or 'frag') your opponent, by reducing his life points to zero, as many times as possible.

This game pack actually includes 5 different games. The cards depict animals which match the figures. In the game which uses the CD, one's CD player is put into random mode and players must be the first to grab the animal who matches the sound.

Components include 18 figures, 2 bells, 20 cards, 1 die, 1 sack 1 musical compact disc.

Test your movie knowledge in questions on films from the 1960's to present, this trivia game asks questions in various forms from straight knowledge of films to "first person" who-am-I. If you get the question right you get to roll again. There are some variations listed that punish you for wrong answers by moving back spaces moved forward in that roll and giving other players the chance to steal if incorrectly answered incorrectly.

Start Trek: The Motion Picture license published in book form with punch out pieces.

The rules state that the goal is to circle the ship spaces on the board until you get six "deflector shields." Then you are supposed to go up the "special corridor" spaces that lead to the bridge. The first player to reach the bridge wins.

Unfortunately, I see no "special corridor" spaces that lead to the bridge, so this game is broken right from the outset.

_____________________________________

In fact, this is a misprint on the cover and in most copies of the game board that I've seen (including my own). There is supposed to be a "ladder" of three spaces between the TURBO-ELEVATOR space and the Bridge image in the center of the board making up the "special corridor", as (admittedly) very vaguely described in the last paragraph of the rules. (Seeing as how the rules DO specify starting on the REC DECK space, which is just to the left of the TURBO-ELEVATOR space, this presumes that movement around the board is in a clockwise method--something again NOT specified in the rules.)

This missing "corridor" can be drawn in with a white or metallic-ink paint marker as shown in the photo of the board in the IMAGES section. Apparently, someone caught this toward the end of the book's run, and some of the gameboards have this fixed.

A revised set of rules to clarify these points are also available in the FILES section.

From the box:
"An intricate challenge to build and create winning sequences of colors and combinations. Strategy builds as players place their colored beads. Find a winning combination before your opponent."

A sequence/pattern is decided on by all the players.  The players then attempt to create this pattern by either placing new colored beads on the board, or by moving other previously played beads.

7 cards are drawn from a deck of 54 cards (with three letters printed on the cards) and placed face up on the table. Players must create words using as many of the letters as possible in 2 minutes.  Bonus points are awarded for longer words and for using all 21 letters.  Winner is the first one to an agreed point total.

From the Game Box:

Who plays Kwa-Dō?  Spirited, Fun-loving players have been challenged by the historical beauty and design of Kwa-Dō for over 400 years!  Kwa-Dō is an exciting, easy-to-learn matching game ideally suited for up to six players - ages 8 to 100+ years.

Players capture points by "matching up" cards whose brightly patterned images reflect the seasons and months of the calendar year.  There are 48 playing cards in the Kwa-Dō deck.

Point cards consist of consist of colorful red and blue scrolls, wild birds and animals, and a mystical dragon.  The dragon is the highest point card in the game.  Players strive to “match” these valuable point cards during their turn, thus preventing opponents an opportunity to score.  Most games can easily be played in less than an hour.

An illustrated rule book and score pad are included with each game.

Taken from the publisher's website:

In Boom Box you are challenged in balancing your Boom Box right and left audio channels. Boom Box can be played by 2 or more players at the same time: each player just needs the supplied deck of 27 cards (print and cut them); the faster the player gets rid of his cards, the better! The cards represent different notes in three different colours and different lengths. After each player has flipped over the first card from their decks (this card representing the Boom Box itself), they must play their cards to the left or right of the radio, respecting these two easy rules: if you want to place a card on the Boom Box left channel, this card does not have to have any characteristic in common - number of notes, colour, length - with the preceding one; on the other hand, if you want to place a card on the Boom Box right channel, it must have at least one thing in common with the previous one. It is easy, isn't it? But what if the goal is to have the same number of cards on the left and on the right? Things get interesting and slightly harder, but funnier as well! Challenge your friends in this fast competition, and you will see! As soon as cards are placed, players draw new ones from their decks: when a deck is emptied, the round is over and points are scored. After three rounds, the player with the most points is the winner.

This game was available in the free web magazine GiocAreA OnLine (former partner of daVinci Editrice) as a promotional tool.

FreeStyle is a game of competition in a swimming pool. Players (from 2 to 8) move their athletes on the board representing a pool: athletes must dive, swim to the other side and back, just as in a real competition. In order to play FreeStyle, you need, beside the board supplied with the file, six dice and a token for each player. FreeStyle has some basic rules, intended for children, and some advanced ones, for adults and children smarter than adults.

Taken from the publisher's website:

CromoGnomo is a game for four players, playing in two teams of two players each, that deals with four painter gnomes.  Each gnome/player begins the game with the same hand of twelve "colour" cards. During his turn, each player either plays in the bucket one of his card, or passes. The colours in the bucket blend to form other colours: at the end of each round, one of the team may score points if the resulting colour is one of his favourites.

An elegant and fun card game created during the event "Create a Game from Scratch" (Gradara Ludens 2003) by:  Andrea Angiolino, Roberto Cestaro, Claudio Del Pizzo, Emiliano Liverani, Giovanni Galanti, Silvano Sorrentino.

This game was available in the free web magazine GiocAreA OnLine (former partner of daVinci Editrice) as a promotional tool.

Taken from the publisher's website:

The Artifact takes us in a fantasy world far away, where humans, magical and evil creatures can be easily found walking around, just as in any Tolkien-inspired game. Players take up the role of one of these heroes (a Human, a Dwarf, an Elf and a Hobbit, indeed) and must recover their weapon, which is proved to be the only mean to destroy a powerful Artifact that is trying to put under its control the whole world. The path to the Mountain of Fate, the place where the Artifact is giving out its will, is full of dangerous creatures such as Trolls, Orcs and the mighty Black Knight. During his turn, the player rolls two different colored six-sided dice, thus determining both his hero movement and which of the evil character will be moved by the player sitting to his right. You got it: players are trying both to win advantage for their heroes and to stop the other players' ones at the same time. And if it's not your lucky day, you may even change the outcome you have rolled, but are you ready to pay its price, giving your opponents a free move? The game gets even more interesting because each evil character has its own special ability, and the map represents different types of terrain: each hero has a favored type of terrain (e.g. woods for the Elf) where he can peacefully rest and plan his next moves. Last but not least, there is also the possibility of casting a powerful spell and then bend evil characters' wills to yours...

This game was available in the free web magazine GiocAreA OnLine (former partner of daVinci Editrice) as a promotional tool.

Taken from the publisher's website:

To play PLUG INN, the biggest board game in the world, you need a friend and a big, BIG table. If you don't have a big enough table, fear not! The game board can be easily adapted to the size of your choice... In PLUG INN, players compete in a "Macro-Game" by placing their tokens on a board composed of hexagons. Every hex of the game contains a "micro-game" that a player must win in order to place a token in the hex. Macro and micro games are controlled by a few general rules. Even if the general rules do not change, it is always possible to pick up a new Macro-Game or add new micro-games to refresh PLUG INN.

The first supplied Macro-Game is Hex. In Hex, you need to place several micro-games hexes in order to form a diamond-shaped board. Each player marks two opposite sides of the diamond with his tokens. Afterwards, tokens are played in the hexes according to the rules of PLUG INN. The goal is to connect your opposite sides with an uninterrupted chain of your tokens.

The first micro-games are:
-Trip, an interesting variant of the classic Tic-Tac-Toe
-Kono, a simple game in which you need to capture your opponent's tokens
-Bulo, a reduced variant for the classic game of Nim
-Decapawn, inspired by Martin Gardner's Hexapawn, where pawns battle on the board much like in Chess

This game was available in the free web magazine GiocAreA OnLine (former partner of daVinci Editrice) as a promotional tool.

A fast playing card game like Go Fish but with pictures of popular World War II planes in Europe. A group of 4 cards of the same letter group is called a Squadron. One player asks for a particular card from one player. If successful another request can be made. Once a squadron is completed they are placed face up on the table and are out of play. When all players are out of cards the game is over. The player with the most squadrons is the Wing Commander and winner.

8 ten-sided discs have letters on each face; the consonants have points values. The discs are magnetic and hold together, allowing the player to quickly rotate the discs back and forth to make words. You can easily break the whole thing apart to re-arrange the discs for variety. Includes various rules for different word games.

From the rules:  The Slime Monster has invaded the town.  You've got to stop him!  You must get from the High School to the Armory, pick up a land mine and knock the Slime Monster over.  But be careful that the Slime monster doesn't knock you over with his slime.

Movement is directed with a spinner. Each spin is used twice: once for the player and the same amount for the monster.  The players try to manipulate the monster so that he winds up standing over an opponent.  He then "slimes" the player and moves.  If the slime covering the player causes the player piece to fall over, the player must start again.  Yuck!

The Squirt Game!  A very unusual game that has a water gun attached to the game board.  Spin the spinner on the game board and fire the water gun, but it only fires once or twice out of every six trigger pulls.  Players start the game with 5 tokens.  Trade in small water drip tokens to avoid getting shot and collect tokens when the trigger is pulled but does not fire.  Tokens are small, medium and large sized plastic "water drops."  Trade in 5 small tokens for a medium token.  Trade in 2 medium tokens for a large token and win the game by trading in 2 large tokens for the "Big Shot" button.  Just the game we want to play in a room filled with cardboard game boxes!

Buying and selling of pot in a Monopoly-like game where you want bales of pot rather than Hotels.  The game board and the "Bum Me Out" and "Far Out" cards have colorful cartoon art, but the Pot (property) cards have color photos of the different types of pot you can own.  Simple game with little substance.  Great for collectors of "contraband games," but doesn't get played very often.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Fleet Action is a game concerning large struggles in the Babylon 5 Universe.

From the cover:
Fleet Action is a fast-paced game of starship combat on a fleet scale. No longer are you simply concerned with the actions of just one or two warships. Now you must guide the actions of three dozen at once.

Reiner Knizia's paperback book about dice games runs to 224 pages including the index. There are 8 chapters:
1. Lucky Scoring Games
2. Lucky Counter Games
3. The Theory of Dice (9 pages headed "It's easier than you think")
4. Betting Games
5. Progression Games
6. Jeopardy Games
7. Category Games
8. Bluffing Games

There are tons of games here, all explained pretty simply, in an open, friendly style. Most have one or two pages and include variants, tactics and odds where applicable. The only illustrations are relevant but basic diagrams, nothing big enough to photocopy.

The front piece notes some games are original to Knizia, and these are the ones that are mentioned as "original" games in the book - games like Catego or Six Hundred.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
from thr MGDG website:

By the beginning of 1954, both French and Viet Minh realized that the war in Indochina was nearing an end. Ho Chi Minh had requested cease-fire negotiations in Geneva, Switzerland, and the French government was looking for a way out that would give them peace with honor. Both sides felt that a "big win" on the battlefield would finally secure at the peace table what they had been unable to obtain in six and a half years of war.

The site of this battle would be near a village called, in Vietnamese, "Seat of the Border County Prefecture" located in a distant valley of north-western Vietnam near the Laotian border. The French high command, under General Henri Navarre, reasoned that such remote location could not be successfully attacked. General Vo Nguyen Giap thought the position was nearly impossible to defend. The siege of Dien Bien Phu would be the result.

Vallée de la mort is a simulation of this epic siege that ended the French colonial presence in Asia and marked the beginning of a 21-year struggle for national unification. The game is designed for 2 players, one taking the role of the French commander of the fortress complex at Dien Bien Phu, the other of General Giap commanding the Viet-Minh forces laying siege. The game and scenarios are small enough to be played solitaire, however.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: This unique deck of cards uses snaps and pairs to teach children various concepts of time.  Features include a 24 hour clock, digital clock, roman clock face, a.m. and p.m., etc...  Ages 4 and up
family: Series: Educational Snap and Pairs (Green Board Game Co)

description: Players race to complete their animals by trading cards.  Their are 15 animals to complete, each with three parts.  Ages 4 and up
Time's Table Snap uses a unique deck of cards to teach children multiplication concepts through games of pairs and snaps.  Great for children practicing their multiplication tables. Ages 6 and up
family: Series: Educational Snap and Pairs (Green Board Game Co)

description:
A unique deck of cards designed to teach the concept of fractions using snaps and pairs.  Covers percentages, decimals, and other fraction concepts.  Includes a self-checking guide.  Ages 6 and up

Uses the basic rules of the card game of War (flip a card, highest wins - ties flip another card with the winner getting them all). Game is played until one player has all of the cards.

A unique card deck designed to teach children children basic math concepts using snaps and pairs.  Covers addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, as well as a few square roots, squares, and cubes.  Ages 7 and up
family: Series: Educational Snap and Pairs (Green Board Game Co)

description: All the players are dealt 5-8 cards, depending on the number of people playing. Each card lists a thing to which a person is likely to have some emotional response. The cards have things as varied as "hip hop," "the smell of oranges," "Brad Pitt," "scoring the winning point," and "breakfast in bed." One player is the Lumper. The other players must give the Lumper a card from their hand that they think he/she will like a lot. The Lumper then arranges the cards on a board, which has spaces for Like It (10 points), Thumbs Up (7 points) A-OK (5 points), Not Bad (3 points), So So (2 points), Whatever (1 point), and Lump It (negative 5 points). The players earn (or lose) points depending on where their card was placed. The role of Lumper moves to the left, and when everyone has been a Lumper a prescribed number of times, the game ends. The highest score wins.

Taken from the publisher's website:

The cost of life is increasing every day, and you do not know what to do with the coins in your pockets? Then Arena is the game for you! All the matches in this particular Arena are fought using nothing but coins and some dexterity: the White player and the Black one, on their turn, grab one of the twenty coins available and drop it in the Arena, trying to cover their opponent's dots while protecting their owns. Each move can mess up the board, because you are not only challenging your friend, but also the force of gravity itself!

This game was available in the free web magazine GiocAreA OnLine (former partner of daVinci Editrice) as a promotional tool.

Tactical, topological tile game. It's set in medieval Northern Germany where four merchants set off to sell their goods in other towns and have to discover the safest and shortest roads for themselves, thereby avoiding many obstacles and roughness.

Two-player abstract played on an 8x8 grid.  The basic pieces are tall columns belonging to either player.  When a player jumps another player's column, he places one of his discs on it, claiming it for himself.  Columns can change ownership repeatedly in this way.  The winner is the first player to move his remaining columns into his opponent's home row.

"Can a game with sixteen pieces and simple rules provide the action of checkers and the strategy of chess?  The answer is yes, if the game is Pounce, the game that takes minutes to learn--and years to master."

In the far off distant future, corporations vie for control of new worlds and the resources they comprise. Using five 8-sided dice printed with colorful icons, players vie to land explorers, miners, bases, and supply ships on a newly-discovered planet. Watch out, though - your opponent's dice can knock into yours, disrupting your carefully laid explorations!

You need to print and assemble the dice yourself. Paper is OK, but 110lb cardstock is better. Surprisingly, once folded and glued, the dice have a fair bit of weight to them - not enough to put someone's eye out, but enough for the game to be playable.

The commercial PDF also included a cut-n-assemble box to keep the components in once you've cut and folded. The free version does not.

All-in-the-Dice Baseball is an exciting dice game that simulates all of the action found in professional baseball. There are hits, outs, walks, steals, and erros. Although the results of each play are in the dice, it is the player who determines the strategy to be employed. The game has been statistically perfected so that it is accurate within 2% of professional baseball. Anyone with an understanding of the game of baseball will have endless hours of fun playing the game. AITD can be played with 1 or 2 players. The rules are below in the image section.

"Bridgehead is a game of military tactics a two teams maneuver to build and control a bridge of men across the board between two base positions."

Simple abstract game with a 5x8 position board and 6 pieces per side.

Players take turns rolling the dice and collecting tokens representing spirit, loyalty, pride and girl power.  The first player to get ten girl power tokens gets to decide to take a solo win, or to continue the game until everyone has ten girl power (a group win).  The various spaces and cards all reflect scenarios tending to stress the game's unabashed message of cooperative endeavor.

boardgame: WarCraft: The Board Game – Expansion Set
description:
Only the Ruthless Survive!

Warcraft: The Board Game receives its highly-anticipated expansion! The Warcraft Expansion Set includes a multitude of exciting and flavorful new options for your Warcraft board game experience.

* New rules and components for the sixteen Warcraft heroes
    * Random “creep” encounters
    * New special race decks
    * New specialized board tiles
    * New scenarios
    * New resource system

For countless hours of brutal fantasy fun!

The Warcraft Expansion Set requires WarCraft: The Board Game to play.

Expands:

    WarCraft: The Board Game
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Zamitar is a chess style game with very 70’s inspired sci-fi acrylic pieces. Each piece has it’s own move, as in a normal chess game. The difference being that a few of the pieces can bounce their moves off the walls.
To win you must take the opponents Space Station piece. The astronaut piece definitely has a “Danger - Will Robinson” (from the old LOST IN SPACE TV Show)  feel about it. It is possible to purchase two other forces so you can have a four-player version.

Crosshand Poker is a cross between scrabble and poker.
108 (two decks including jokers) traditional playing-card symbol tiles being played on a scrabble-esque grid board inclusive of double and triple scores.

Players draw and update 5 tiles in their rack and play poker 'hands' of tiles branching from the center of the board. Each type of hand has a different score as set out in the. Very much like scrabble except the scores are not on a per-tile basis. 
Winner has most points when pool of tiles is depleted.

Alphabet SQUIGGLE™ introduces letters to kids in a fun and clever new way. Kids will learn and improve letter recognition, stimulate their creativity and even strengthen their vocabulary. Children who are just learning letters and those who already know them well will enjoy playing. Watch how surprised they get when they see how much they can do with letters.

Players move along a path of letters. For every letter they land on, the players must find that letter on the outside edge of the board and either trace it or write it from memory on the sketch paper provided. If they're feeling creative, they can turn their letter tracing into a picture, write a word that begins with that letter, or create a drawing that begins with and reinforces that letter.

Roll the die and move around the board. If you land on a letter square, draw a card with a long word on it. You have 60 seconds to find the longest word using the letters in the card word. You move a variable number of spaces depending on the length of the found word. The other players can challenge you by spelling out a longer word. There is a small dictionary that lists all spellable words for each card word. Game includes over 400 word cards, 25 tokens for scoring, 1 d6 and a 1 min sand timer.

From the box: Three Blind Dice combines the excitement of a sharp shooting dice game with the thrill of a high stakes card game. Out smart other players in an attempt to keep your dice. Out bluff the same players into losing their dice. However beware! One wrong bid and you lose a die, lose all five and the game is over.
The aim of the game is for players to get all aircraft into the airport and airborne again as quickly as possible to beat the other players.

Abstract game on 6x6 grid that folds up to form the box. Players have 6 pieces each with one side black, the other white, like Reversi/Othello pieces. Pieces may move in any direction and when they jump an opponents piece, it's turned over to show your color. 
An uninspiring variant on Reversi which lacks clear rules. Can you make multiple jumps? How far can you move? Can you make a better game with the contents?

A children's game where the object is to be the last player in the game.  A plastic and cardboard boat (divided into colored quarters) is balanced on the corners of the box.  A spinner dictates the placement of a pirate mouse on a colored section of the boat.  If all the pirates are placed onto the boat, the spinner then dictates which pirates are removed from the boat.  A players that causes the pirates to fall into the ocean (box) is eliminated from the game.  The remaining player is declared the winner.  Game art was done by Richard Scarry.

Each of the two players is a captain of a team.  The team is represented by 11 wood units with a peg on them where a wood ball may sit.  The soccer field is represented by circle spaces for movement.  The active player rolls 2d6 to determine movement allowed.  Movement allowance is spread out as desired between your players and determined by whether a player has the ball or not.  It's all very simple, but works fantastic!   Currently out of print.
Its a Race over several Tracks and if you came late, you get more Points. But if you are too late you get no Points.

Railway Rivals Map CH: China, a map for use with both Railway Rivals and Dampfross, covers from Shenyang in the northeast, the Mongolian border in the northwest, the Laos/Vietnamese  border in the southwest, and the Pacific Ocean in the southeast/east.

Help Emily, Bagpuss and friends find homes for the lost things.

This game is based on the children’s TV series, Bagpuss about an old toy cat and his friends; toys that come to life to study the lost things their owner Emily brings into her special shop. Players move around the board taking penalties, extra goes or collecting cards with pictures of one or more of the characters from the series on them. Whenever a player has a complete set of all of the characters they can trade them in for a card from the 'lost things' pile. When all of the lost things have been drawn the player with the most wins.

Easiest description is likely that this game plays similar to Spades.  It uses a regular deck of 52 playing cards, it is about taking tricks to match a 'bid' (assigned in 7-4-1 versus bidding in Spades), with Aces high and a trump suit (declared in 7-4-1 versus always spades in Spades).

In 7-4-1, 12 cards are dealt to each of the 4 players; this leaves a 4-card kitty.  The dealer MUST try to take 7 tricks, but she gets to declare which suit is trump AFTER looking at her hand.  She then gets to pick up the 4 middle cards, and discard 4 face-down (could be from the kitty, or not).  Player 2 must get 4 tricks, player 3 for 1 trick, and player 4 need not take any.

After round 1, figure out how many cards more or less players got.  YOu get one point for every trick you make above your required tricks, and lose one for every trick you fail to make.  For the next round, a player who had MORE in the previous round can then pass one card per amount more to a player who had less; that lesser player must then pass their HIGHEST card in each requested suit.  Some pre-defined ordering for this is necessary; the player needing to make the most tricks has precedence over the player needing less.

For example, in round 1, the players take 6-2-3-1 tricks.
So, the position 2 is now the new dealer.  Player in position 3 was +2 in round 1 (took 3, needed 1), and would ask position 1 for 1 suit, and position 2 for 1 suit.  Player in position 4 would then ask position 2 for 1 suit.  We've played where if the numbers over/under match (position 3&2, 4&1 in this example), then they could simply swap cards too.

Play then proceeds as described above.  Trump is declared AFTER the card-swapping is done.  And if the player who needed 4 in the previous round took NONE, then they can also look at the kitty too before declaring a trump!  However, they'd also have just given up 4 good cards to other players...\
Game ends after either 2 or 3 rounds, with everyone getting a chance at each position an equal number of times.

Load the camel with straw, but don't be the one to add the straw that breaks the camel's back! Two piece camel on wheels, held together with a rubber band, will eventually collapse under the weight of the wooden sticks.

This abstract game consists of five concentric rings divided into sixteen sectors (bars).  Each player has a home sector that is four bars wide.  Ten directional playing pieces start in the center bars of the home sector facing outwards.  Each turn players roll two dice and use each dice roll to move a piece either in its facing direction or along its bar.  When a piece starts a turn outside of it home sector and then is moved within the sector it is removed from the game.  The first player to run out of pieces wins the game.

Additional complexity comes from blocking, capturing (freezing pieces while simultaneously preventing other opposing pieces from entering the bar in which the capture occurs), removing pieces from the board through `ringers' and special moves from rolling doubles.  Variant rules exist for more cutthroat games and games with more than two players.

Campaign Secrets is a political card game where you are a candidate in the race for the White House. You must out-campaign your opponents to win the favor of your constituents. And most importantly, don't let those voters find out about your dirty little secrets!

Campaign Secrets is a fun educational game. Each card has tidbits about real presidential elections. In addition, the campaign strategies and secrets in the game, are all taken from real-life strategies and scandals. Playing the game can give many children and adults alike an insight into how a real election comes together.

In the game, you play a series of campaign strategies trying to get more percentage of the vote than the other players in the game. Eventually, through game play, you'll either choose or stumble into your running mate. Together the two of you must do your best to wrangle as many votes as possible away from the other players, and to hold on to your secrets so you can pick up a few extra votes at the end of the game.

The game was designed for families and classrooms, so while it can be fun and educational for hard-core gamers, it's still simple enough for everybody to play. If you're looking for a fun way to learn about the election process, or just a fun game to play with your family, then this is the game for you. If you're looking a hard-core strategy game then you might want to look elsewhere.

NOTE: You may have played the First Edition of this game, but the Second Edition plays considerably different. The first edition was a quick and dirty version put out as a free give away for trade shows, the second edition is the real game.

From the box:
"Each player has 6 coloured stickers attached to their body.  Turns are taken to throw the giant coloured dice, and a hand or foot is placed on another person's sticker of the colour thrown.
"Before long there is a mass of writhing bodies in the room!
"How embarrassing it gets depends on where people put their stickers!
"CONTENTS: 200 Touch and Feel Stickers, one giant wood dice"
Produced for C&A shops.

Pig Dice, an ancestor of Pass the Pigs and a descendant of Pig, is a jeopardy dice game, where the dominant type of decision is whether or not to jeopardize previous gains by rolling for potential greater gains.  In particular, Pig Dice is a jeopardy race game, where the object is to be the first to meet or exceed a goal score.

One die is a standard die with a pig head replacing the 1.  The other is a standard die with a pig tail replacing the 6.  Such rolls are called Heads and Tails, respectively, and modify the value of the roll.  The goal score is 100, yet after a player has met or exceeded 100, all other players have one more turn to achieve a higher score.

Each collectable Nak Nak figure can be posed in over 80 different stacking poses. Object is to see how many NAKNAK figures you can stack. 
Each Player takes turns rolling 2 dice, one die determines if u stack your Nak Nak on the ground or on the stack on top of another Nak Nak. The other die determines what body part of the Nak Nak that need to be touching the stack or ground. 
If a player drops the stack the player who went before him takes the Nak Naks that fell off the stack, Last player with Nak Naks Wins.

Don't Drop Anything, Little Raven!  How much can the little raven carry?  The small raven is moving house. There are many things from the old nest that must be brought to the new nest. And because the small raven wants to carry everything all at once, players try to attach as many different objects as possible on the raven's figure. The screen over the wing, the pocket to the tail, the cake on the head... Pay attention so that nothing falls off.

Explore the world! You've been transported to a mystery location - now figure out where you are. Race around the board to collect clues...the first player to put clues together and figure out the mystery location wins the game.

Taken from the publisher's website:

In this heavily  armed card game, you take on the role of  a military superpower. Your mission: To  obtain 100 points worth of property. And  we're not talking property like your grandmother's 2 story house, or a Ford  Pinto with 56 thousand miles on it, we  mean Washington D.C., Moscow, and Rome to  name a few.

Game play wise, don't expect to see  the "honorable" warfare that died off in the samurai centuries.  Now-a-days, we've got spies, blackmail,  market manipulation, terrorism, and of  course, the ever popular nuclear bomb,  which has the ability to completely  annihilate any city quicker than you can  yell "HEY! What the?!?!" Now THAT's a great Sunday family activity!

With Global Supremacy, expect to lose  your mind… The speed of the game is  fast, so keep a clear head or you'll lose  it, along with any chances of winning the  game. Sound fun? It does to me, too! So  pick Global Supremacy up, since the price  matches all other products here at  WM… Nothing.

Global Supremacy can be downloaded at whitemask.com

Taken from the publisher's website:

It is time for the great uprising.  Kalik Nevar - Necromancer Lord of the great Undead  Army, has summoned you back to your original human shell.  Unfortunately, it’s been a long, long time  since you’ve walked the mortal planes.  And it’s not like you’ve ever seen your own skeleton before.   And to make matter’s worse, Kalik’s got everything stack up in his great “Mound of Death”.  It’s going to  be a pain in the hip bone to get assembled back together...  and it looks like the other spirits are having the  same problem...

Bone Barter is game of ruthless skeletal trading.  Your mission - to be the first  to rebuild your old body.

Bone Barter can be downloaded at whitemask.com

From the box bottom:

This entertaining information game combines risk-taking and strategy.  A roll of the giant 12-sided die determines the category you'll answer, or which special instruction you'll follow.

If you roll a category, you must decide how much you know in that field.  Is Sports your best category?  Then bet you'll win the maximum $100.  Not sure about Entertainment?  Then select $50.  Totally adrift in Science?  Then try the easiest $25.  Keep in mind you're trying to be the first player to win $750 in game chips.

Each side of each card has three clues, all leading to the same answer.  The clues range from difficult to easy, but all will be informative and fun.  There are 1,200 different answers and 3,600 clues in all, so the game can be played many times.

Special instructions on the die, such as Double or Their Choice, give the game real variety.  As an added twist, you can challenge your opponent if you think he or she has bet more than he or she knows.

A one-on-one fighting game that uses highly articulated, customizable, fully interchangeable figures based on the Stikfas line of figures. Various factions duke it out using customizable dice to determine battle outcome and effect with the players removing parts from their figures as they take damage, and adding back as they heal.
A hybrid of rock-paper-scissors and checkers in which players move pieces of four kinds over a cross-shaped board.

boardgame: No Middle Ground: The Battle for the Golan Heights, October 6 to 10, 1973
description:
No Middle Ground' is a simulation of the Syrian Offensive (Operation Badr) at the beginning of the Yom Kippur War. The Syrians were attempting to breakthrough to the highly populated Israeli territories to the west, as well as drawing Israeli air and ground forces from their Egyptian allies offensive in the Sinai. The game is intended to be an easy-to-learn game suitable for novices, as well as a challenging and fun contest for veteran grognards.

The 11 x 17 map depicts the Golan Heights at 4 km per hex.
The 168 counters represent Israeli battalions and Syrian and Arab-allied units brigades.

Armies of Oblivion is the long-awaited module adding the Axis Minors (Romanian, Hungarian, Bulgarian, Slovakian, Croatian) order of battle to the Advanced Squad Leader tactical game system.
NOTE: Finns are in Beyond Valor: ASL Module 1 and Hakkaa Päälle!: ASL Module 14.

Contents:

    4 8"x22" geomorphic mapboards 48, 49, 50, 51 [all editions]
    Overlays Hi8-Hi12, Rv1, Wd12, Wd34 [all editions]
    Overlays Hi2, Hi5, OG1, P1, RR1, RR2, RR4-6, RR14, X12 [2nd edition only]
    6 Counter sheets [all editions - each edition has different countersheets - see images]
    ASL Scenarios  27-34 [2nd edition only], 111-121 [all editions], 122, 219-230 [2nd edition only] 
    Axis Minor Chapter H pages (H143-H170) [all editions]
    Errata pages A51-A62; D21-D26; S27-S34 (Note that pages A51-A62 and D23-D26 were updated in Hakkaa Päälle!; these updated pages are also included in the 2018 edition) [all editions]
    2 Axis Minor SASL Cards (Generation and Random Event) [all editions]


Scenario List:

[all editions]:
111 Balkan Sideshow, Szenttanas, Yugoslavia, 1941
112 Out of Cowardice, Senta, Yugoslavia, 1941
113 Liberating Bessarabia, Branzeni, Bessarabia, 1941
114 Cautious Crusaders, Lipovic, Ukraine 1941
115 Huns of Steel, Uriv, Russia, 1942
116 The Sixth Blow, Skopce, Poland, 1944
117 With Tigers On Their Tail, Saturnia, Galicia, 1944
118 Downsizing the Uprising, Vrutky, Slovakia, 1944
119 Ancient Feud, Cluj, Transylvania, 1944
120 Return to Sender, Nish, Serbia, 1944

Trhyme (pronounced Trime) is a word game with a triple twist: you're given three clues for which you must provide three answers within a specified time, and the three answers must rhyme with one another.

Example: Birds do it, bees do it, bells do it.
Answer: Sing, Sting, Ring

The first player/team to the center of the board by answering the correct trhymes wins.

boardgame: Save The World: A Cooperative Environmental Game
description:
Save The World is a cooperative board game for 2 to 6 players, in which the players team up to stop all the ecological crises that threaten the earth's survival. Players strategize together and have a 50-50 chance of staving off the end of the world.

--From the designer:
"My lifelong passion is the environment. I've been an alarmist eco-nut since the 60's, when as editor of the Los Angeles Free Press, the grandaddy of the underground press, I became aware of the man-made threats to habitation on the planet. In 1980, I created Save the World, the cooperative environmental board game. Players were confronted with all the threats to the planet and offered all the solutions, with the idea of taking them out into real life.  So far, the game has not achieve its mission. When I started research for Save the World, I didn't think we'd be around this long. After two years of study, I concluded that a small number of humans would survive, and a small number of other species: some consolation, I guess. I'm pleasantly surprised that we're still here, but the long-term prospects are still not encouraging."

Race fans! Your goal is to collect all of the cards that will allow you to run WIDE OPEN and race to the finish line. Roll the dice, hit the right combination and take the checkered flag. It's real racing action! You must qualify to race, roll out of pit stops, and beware of racing hazards that may shift the lead on any turn. Watch out! There's always the chance of a wipe out. Have fun... It's Awesome!

In CARTEL: The International Oil Game, you try to become the wealthiest player, and therefore, the winner. You will try to invest in oil fields throughout the world, and sell any crude oil that you obtain for the most profit. The first player to obtain ten million petro-dollars in cash is the winner of the game.

Note: This is not the same thing as the Philip Orbanes game Cartel.

description:
The game consists of a game board representing the race track at the Carthage Circus, a long, oval wooden piece separating the center of the board and used as a lap counter, 4 wooden chariots, 28 Fates Cards, 45 Chance Cards and 2 dice.

At the start of the game each player selects a color and the Fates Cards are distributed evenly between the players. On a players turn they roll the dice and move that many spaces forwards. On the roll of an even number, movement must be straight ahead. If an an odd number is rolled, the player can change lanes. The game ends once a player has completed 7 laps of the track.

The innovation that stops this from becoming a roll and move game are the Fates Cards and the Chance Cards. The board spaces are marked with either a "C" Chance symbol, an "F" Fates symbol, or a Fatum symbol. If your piece ends its turn in one of the spaces, then you follow the directions. If you land on a Fatum symbol and have the corresponding Fates card then you follow the directions on the Fates card either good or bad. If you land on an "F" symbol then you can swap a Fates card with the player on your left. If you land on a "C" symbol then you take a Chance card and the instructions are followed by the player indicated on the card.

This game is not to be taken too seriously and has a large element of luck.
The most unique thing about this game is that it is a Tunisian production released for the 28th century anniversary since the founding of Carthage, and as such was produced for the tourist market.

Spin and move game based on the 1981 MGM movie, Clash of the Titans has each player taking on the role of Perseus.  Each Perseus is trying to gather the Medusa's head and the five magic items needed to pass the five obstacles in a race to the finish where he will show the Medusa's head to the Kraken and win the game.

description:
OverviewMunchkin Blender expands every card game in the Munchkin family. It's intended for games with multiple base and expansion sets (now known as "blender" games).

				
				
					Publisher's DescriptionStir Things Up!

It's the ultimate party kit for Munchkin fans! Munchkin players really like to combine their sets... Munchkin, Star Munchkin, Munchkin Fu, all in one game. That gets really weird... but it can also slow the game down.

There are a lot of ways we COULD have addressed this problem. But this is Munchkin, so the solution we chose was to create a lot of really overpowered new cards. Why be a Super-Munchkin when you can be an Ultra-Munchkin? If a Half-Breed isn't enough, you can be a 1/3-Breed. And if a single Cheat card doesn't do it for you, you can Cheat With Both Hands!

There are also a lot more monster enhancers... and ITEM enhancers, too. There are several new low-level monsters to help you get those easy kills. Unless somebody takes that Sock Puppet and turns it into the Ultra-Rare Extremely Sneaky Sock Puppet With Extra Cheese . . .

Munchkin till you drop! Munchkin Blender -- just add players!

This is an expansion, not a stand-alone game. It is meant to be combined with at least one Munchkin game, and preferably two or more, with or without other supplements.

				
				
					OtherThis set was updated as part of The Great 2010 Munchkin Changeover but was never reprinted. Many of the updated cards are available, however, via Munchkin: Go Up a Level.

Part of the Munchkin series.

This set is permanently out of print and has been superseded by Munchkin 7: Cheat With Both Hands, which has all the same cards but with the fantasy Munchkin color scheme.

description:
From the Kadon web site:

A gameboard like no other — the grid is a swirl of arches, a network of intersections. Three intriguing games of positional and movement strategy employ a unique element:  a piece flips on every move from slider to jumper and back again. In Endpoint your goal is to exit all 15 of your pieces from the board through the opponent's home row. In End Jam you win if all the opponent's endpoints are occupied. In End Rush each player seeks to capture the board's center space. Some solitaire puzzles are also included. Cylindrical wood playing pieces have felt on one side to indicate their status. The 24" gameboard is offered in two formats, recommended for Ages 10 and up.

Two teams try to describe and guess ideas - nothing could be simpler!

Or perhaps not,  as each player in the team must say one word in turn.  And his word and those of his fellow team members must make a grammatically correct sentence.  Not so easy if each person is thinking along different lines!  For each correct answer,  that team's playing token moves nearer the goal.

You are a guest on the Jerry Springer Show who has temporary amnesia after a wild on-stage scuffle. You can't remember who you are. By using the insults and various other clues provided by other players, the first to figure out exactly who the (bleep) they are will be the winner

From the old time television show of the 1950's. (remember Dorothy Killgalen and Bennett Cerf!).

That's one down and nine to go.... From the show that coined the phrase, "is it bigger than a breadbox?" What's My Line is the classic guessing game. Players ask yes or no questions to unravel the unusual occupation of the guest contestant. Get a no answer and play moves on to the next guesser. With photos of actual people, a TV screen to put them in, and blindfolds like the ones on the classic game show, What's My Line is destined to evoke memories, laughter and hours of endless fun.
Rummy game with a Hawaiian-fish theme.

boardgame: Skillcat: The Nine Lives Card Game
description:
Trick taking card game (capture a specific amount),with new scoring system ( 1st-4th place in bid accuracy). No more great cards dealt determining winner here, as each player has control over his own scoring. Each player has 9 lives to begin game,if you lose them all you are replaced by a Computer player and game continues. (internet version) Game play is 6-12 hands, approx.20-40 minutes
CP has exact rules of play. Game has 6 tie-breakers, as scoring is close (12-10-8-6)Also has a"halo" rule. Game is singles play, no partnerships, spades always trump suit. web add is www.skillcat.com

Every Player (Patient) get 4 Symptom Cards that show their Brain disease. 
On the Symptom Cards is a List of Treatments that you must take step by step from the Top to the Bottom to heal the disease. 
To heal you must visit some Places in turn (First the Doctor,Then the Drugstore, ect...)and pay there Money for the Treatment.If more than one Card need the same Treatment at the same Time, you must only pay once for all same Treatments. That save Time and Money.
To win the Game you must Cure all 4 Diseases.

 1 Gameboard

54 Brainstorm Cards (17 Good,17 Bad)
A lot of Game money

Swastika was published in 1907.  It is described as a "popular new Indian card game."  The deck of cards is composed of 55 cards in five suits:  bows, arrows, peace pipes, tomahawks, and swastikas.  (The swastika is an American Indian symbol with lots of history before its use by Nazi Germany.)  Rules and photos to be posted.

a central board

to match the heads, bodies and tails to score the number of points shown on the bodies.

An entertaining game for children which plays very similar to Carcassonne. Place a tile wherever it matches and claim the points.
I introduced the variant of providing each player with a counter, so they can claim one bug as their own to be finished later, like in Carcassonne. That way, players are not exposed to be stolen their bugs and encouraged to build longer bugs.

It turns to be quite fun due to the pictures and also when any child by mistake place two heads or two tails together....  X-Mutant bugs!!

gameboard

To complete your story book and with your special key, unlock the correct door to find your handsome prince and live happily ever after!

Every girl (this game is only for girls as you try to reach for your prince!) start the journey around the gameboard collecting all the pages of your one special story. 
Once you have completed your storybook you can open the secret compartment to find your key.

With the key in your hand, race to reach the right tower door as quickly as possible. Your key will only fit one lock... only when you open the lock will the door be opened, uniting you and your prince forever!

I bought this game for a present. The bits, castle and board are very nice, so I decided to add the entry. It will only appeal to 5-8 years old girls.

CONTENTS:
colorful board
plastic transparent over board with holes to hold the spinners

Each player takes 3 tops of the same color and spins one top per turn from his colored bay, which is his starting area.
The tops will stop in one of the numbered holes (2-3-4 o 5)
The players write down their scores after the 3 turns and start all over again.

You can play to 100, 200 or more points.

Playing board
4x player pieces (boys) with bases and 1 witch

Collect the 3 particular ingredients you need from the creepy cauldron (as listed in the spell book in your home base); once you have them, obtain the key from the mouth of the ugly witch's cauldron and make it to the exit.

To race the 4 frogs from one home to the other.

PLAY
The starting leaf of player A is the finishing leave of B and vice versa. While all the small leaves lay in between as a path... quite pretty effect.

The movement is indicated by the number on each frog. Therefore the frog of value 1 will move or jump 1, the frog with a 4 will move or jump 4 leaves.

There is not luck involved, all the moves take place on that way. A frog is allowed to leap over other frogs and only unoccupied leaves will count as a move. Frogs can also knock off opponent's frogs when jumping over.

From the box:

"Like the popular NOVA television program on PBS, the NOVA TRUE SCIENCE game will intrigue and entertaining science-phobes and science-philes alike. Players wander around a Big Brain Game Board and earn points by answering simple true-false questions or tougher "genius" questions. The 900 questions fall into six science categories: Life, Earth, Physics, Technology, Science & Society, and Random Access (anything goes). Players can use Brain Boost and Cross Current spaces to take strategic shortcuts. Plus, they can even bet on their opponent's science knowledge by taking an Einstein's Gamble."

Game includes Big Brain Game Board, 4 playing pieces, 200 True/False cards, 100 Genius cards, 1 die, and Rule Booklet which includes a brief glossary of science terms.

A joint effort by Aristoplay and WGBH-Boston Educational Foundation, producers of NOVA.

This game needs people who know people! How many women would rather be a man? How many singles go to a party with a condom in their pocket? Spicy questions ensure heated debate and exciting times! That the discussion should turn to the likes and dislikes of the players themselves, that's hard to avoid...

boardgame: Dwarf Mountain: A Game of Fantasy Adventure
description:
An old-school fantasy dungeon crawl / skirmish game from the Grenadier "Great Little Game Line." A troop of dwarfs, aided by the wizard Palintar, face off against the evil Demonicus and his Slaves of Evil. The components include a colorful 16x16" folding map, a CRT and spell tables sheet, score/log pads, and cut-'em-yourself counters. Abbreviated solitaire rules are included.

From Grenadier Models: "Dwarf Mountain: A fantasy game for all ages, featuring a band of feisty dwarfs attempting to recapture their enchanted ancient homestead from a group of nasty characters led by the evil Demonicus. Can the hardy little group overcome the obstacles they will meet as they go deeper and deeper into the rock carved passages to rid their mountain of evil? Dwarf Mountain features a full color playing board, cut counters, rules booklet, and printed score sheets."

description: Players take turns placing wooden planks of varying length onto a 10x11 square grid. Each player plays planks of one color. Neutral planks may be distributed on the grid prior to the game, creating variable starting positions. Players' planks may be played directly onto the gameboard, or on top of other planks at equal height. Planks may intersect over only one square, and a plank must be played on top of at least one plank of its own color. The game ends when neither player can place further planks; scores are assessed according to the area enclosed by each player's planks. Because it is often advantageous to place last, matches are played in pairs where each player goes first once.

From the publisher's website:

Legends of Syrrith is a boardgame for up to 4 players, with playing time usually being 1-2 hours. The boardgame can be played solitaire, as a group or you can compete against other players.

In solitaire and competition mode players start the game with a single hero, one henchmen and a small amount of gold. When played as a group each player chooses a hero that they wish to control and the group works as a team to accomplish missions and encounters.

As heroes explore Syrrith they will gain powerful items, treasure and additional heroes to join their group. With over 30 heroes and henchmen to pick from, not to mention over 100 different monsters, traps, dungeons and encounters each game will always be interesting. As each players group grows in power they will be able to take on stronger opponents and harder dungeons.

Legends of Syrrith can be played by itself as a full stand alone game or it can even be used with the Shadow Wars boardgame. When used with the Shadow Wars boardgame, this expansion takes on a whole new twist. Many Heroes can be used in your armies and the items that your adventurers find are useful in your goal to conquer Syrrith. Heroic warriors, arcane wizards, crafty rogues and ancient items of power will be at your command.

In "Barbarians" one player plays the role of a great Roman Emperor ruling the Roman Empire in the period that goes from its apex to its fall, facing the invasions of Barbarians controlled by the other players. The barbarian players act as Attila, Alarico, or other great Barbarian leaders, looking to spread their population over the rich Roman territories and conquer them with their tribes. Each turn represents 25 years of history, during which each Emperor much pay existing legions, recruit new ones and launch his armies into the conquest of Barbarian provinces. Meanwhile, the Barbarian tribes multiply to produce new warriors to tighten their hold and attempt to enlarge their territories.

boardgame: The Wizard of Oz Family Board Game
description:
Box Description: 100 years have past (sic) since L. Frank Baum first told this classic American tale, capturing the imagination of millions.

Pick a character and relive their fantasy as you journey through the enchanted Land of OZ.

Step carefully along the Yellow Brick Road, soar to the Emerald City with the Flying Monkeys, or dare to enter the Wicked Witch's Castle.

With the flip of a card, and the call of "OZ" you may find yourself rich with emeralds! But beware of the Wicked Witch, or your treasure will be gone!

This family game never plays the same way twice. Whether played by adults or children, everyone will be challenged, ensuring the Wizard of Oz will become a classic game your family will enjoy time after time.

The object of Earth One is to be the first to to complete one complete suit.

The deck consists of 60 hexagonal cards, 12 of each suit (colour), 1 wildcard (Starbender) and 11 purchase cards (Zambuk). Each suit consists of one Level 1 card (Hexrex), 2 level 2 cards (Commander and Chancellor), 4 level 3 cards (Skyrine) and 5 level 4 cards (Dade).

To win, a player must table the Hexrex, Commander, Chancellor, 3 Skyrine's and 4 Dade's of the same colour.

The object of the game is to rid one's hand of all cards and not be caught holding the crocodile card.

Includes an oversized deck of attractive Australian animal and gumtree themed cards plus special Bushfire, Crocodile and Hunter cards.

OBJECT: To win all the cards you need to capture Jak, Earl of Evil, by being the first to slap your hand over his picture.

THE PLAY: Each player in turn puts a card face up in the center of the table.  The first player to find Jak, slaps the card and collects all other cards in the center pile.

There are 8 different kinds of cards, each with a picture of a character.  These are original Sci/Fi - Fantasy type characters in the same vein as "He-Man and the Masters of the Universe" or "Krull", although the "Corel the Leader of Good" card has a more distinguished "Battlestar Galactica" look.

A sort of amalgam of Monopoly and Halma, where the objective was to collect as many Jews as you could, and get them off the board. The pieces were little pawns wearing pointed medieval Jewish hats; the players moved them by rolling dice; the child winning was the one whose Jews scurried out, 'off to Palestine!' through the gates of a walled city.

Each player is a Roman noble trying to attain the position of Consul, and enjoying themselves as much as possible along the way. To win, the noble must gain sufficient wealth -at least 150,000 sesterces, 6 slaves, 1 villa and 1 chariot- only then can the noble travel up the "Consul Route" (on the board) to claim the Consul's laurel crown (Hosts may like to provide one)- by throwing a six on one dice.

Listed as 2-6 players in the Second edition, the First edition featured at least eight player tokens.

From the rules:

In the game of Orgy, players take the part of wealthy Roman nobles living in the heyday of the Roman Empire. These nobles would compete for the powerful and prestigious title of Consul. However, pampered by slaves, often intemperate and gluttonous, they enjoyed a hedonistic life-style and plentiful supplies of rich provender that were the spoils of conquest. Players are encouraged to enter into the spirit of the game - Roman dinners could be anything from a frugal meal to a riotous party.

Players move around the board as a result of dice rolls and following the instructions on the square on which the piece stops.Players aim to become the Consul by gaining wealth, 6 slaves, 1 villa and 1 chariot before moving up "The Consul Route".

From the game box: 
BUSTED is a game about selling marijuana on the streets.  A player will find himself in a situation similar to that of a marijuana dealer.

Day of Destiny

When each player reaches the end of his dealing career, his destiny will either be death, because of bad money management; the Slammer, because of his bad luck; or Marijuana Mountain because of his good fortune in dealing.  The last remaining dealer in the game will automatically go to Marijuana Mountain.  Mr. Big feels that you've learned a lesson from all the players and will allow you to turn in all your money and marijuana and go to the Mountain alive.

Race to Build Your Temple Before the Volcano Erupts.

The Angry Rumble of the Erupting Volcano,the Cascade of Boulders and the Ensuing Threat to Buildings Are Captured in This Exciting Action Game."

Push down the Volcano centre piece. Players then take it in turns to take a building piece. Check the value on the piece. Place the correct number of balls into the crater. One ball too many and the volcano will erupt. The erupting volcano may destroy your temple.

The first player to build their temple wins the game.

Meeting of the Minds is an educational board game specifically designed to teach students important knowledge and skills in conflict resolution.  In the game, students answer questions about conflict resolution and must use a win-win strategy to resolve conflicts.  It is designed for students in grades 5 - 8.

Players earn points in two ways.  They can earn points by answering questions correctly.  These questions come up when the players pick "Conflict Resolution" and "True-Flase" cards.  The game creates conflicts among the players and they can also win points by successfully resolving these conflicts.  As a result, the players are involved in two learning processes simultaneously - (1) learning the knowledge and skills for successful conflict resolution and (2) applying the knowledge and skills while playing the game.  Players are therefore cooperating in finding solutions and competing at the same time.  This dynamic relationship leads to a very exciting game.

(Taken from the rules book)

A trivia game for all those who enjoy waxing nostalgic. Question categories include radio, tv, movies, sports, and misc.  Players may also create their own customized categories and questions for their friends and family.

In the Benji Game, players are rolling dice to attempt to get their wooden token to the middle of the board.

Players take turns rolling two dice and moving. If they land on a square with a picture, they either take another turn, roll again, go back to the start or draw a card. The cards aid to manipulate the movement of the pieces.

The first person to reach the center of the board by exact throw is the winner.

2 to 6 players ages 5 to 10

Players spin and move around the board trying not to get bent out of shape.  Includes a body bender marker to make this easier.  Ages 6 and up
